issue_title: "key "stride" not found" when using to_graphviz with Convolutional layers in Julia API
issue_body:
From the Julia API, we can't access the attr "stride" for Convolution layers. 

I reported this issue in the Julia API repo  @pluskid suggested this is may be a bug in the core mxnet API (https://github.com/dmlc/MXNet.jl/issues/213#issuecomment-285790634).

## Environment info
Operating System: ubuntu 14.04

Compiler: gcc (Ubuntu 4.8.4-2ubuntu1~14.04.3) 4.8.4

Package used (Python/R/Scala/Julia): Julia: Version 0.5.0 (2016-09-19 18:14 UTC), Official http://julialang.org/ release, x86_64-pc-linux-gnu

MXNet version:  julia> Pkg.status("MXNet")       0.2.1              e599d51d


## Error Message:
```
ERROR: LoadError: KeyError: key "stride" not found
 in getindex(::Dict{String,Any}, ::String) at ./dict.jl:688
 in #to_graphviz#6566(::String, ::Void, ::Function, ::MXNet.mx.SymbolicNode) at ~/.julia/v0.5/MXNet/src/visualize.jl:71
```


## Minimum reproducible example
```
using MXNet
mlp = @mx.chain mx.Variable(:data)             =>
  mx.Convolution(kernel=(5,5), num_filter=64)
mx.to_graphviz(mlp)
```

The error appears when accessing `node["attr"]["stride"]` when `node["op"]=="Convolution"`.
Accessing `kernel` or `num_filter` works fine.

Printing the node from julia you get:
```
Dict{String,Any}(Pair{String,Any}("name","convolution1"),Pair{String,Any}("attr",Dict{String,Any}(Pair{String,Any}("num_filter","64"),Pair{String,Any}("kernel","(5,5)"))),Pair{String,Any}("op","Convolution"),Pair{String,Any}("inputs",Any[Any[10,0,0],Any[11,0,0],Any[12,0,0]]))
```

## What have you tried to solve it?
Obviously removing the access to the attr in https://github.com/dmlc/MXNet.jl/blob/master/src/visualize.jl#L72-L74, makes the error go away but then there's no stride info in the graph.

Apparently the python api does the same thing as Julia's:
https://github.com/dmlc/mxnet/blob/master/python/mxnet/visualization.py#L252

issue_title: The optimizer object may not properly initialized
issue_body:
Recently I run the tutorial  [Handwritten Digit Recognition](http://mxnet.io/tutorials/python/mnist.html), 
if I change the optimizer option to a optimizer object as following
```
optimizer = mx.optimizer.SGD(learning_rate=0.1)
model = mx.model.FeedForward(
    symbol = mlp,       # network structure
    num_epoch = 10,     # number of data passes for training 
   optimizer = optimizer
)
```
I got poor result. I find that the optimizer class did not set the  mini-batch optimization parameter _rescale_grad_  to (1.0/batch_size).  The correct code is
```
optimizer = mx.optimizer.SGD(learning_rate=0.1, rescale_grad=(1.0/batch_size))
model = mx.model.FeedForward(
    symbol = mlp,       # network structure
    num_epoch = 10,     # number of data passes for training 
   optimizer = optimizer
)
```
Is this an intended design?

issue_title: setting OMP_NUM_THREADS doesn't improve performance with MKL2017
issue_body: I am trying to enable multi-threading by setting the OMP_NUM_THREADS environment variable. The prediction time is improved when I use mxnet built with openblas, however it has no effects when I use mxnet built with MKL. Anyone knows why? And what is the correct way to turn on multi-threading with MKL? Thanks!

issue_title: How to identify whether the content of the  rec files for training are correctly generated?
issue_body:
I train the vgg on the MS-Celeb-1M, finding that it doest's converge at all.
I'm convinced that it has something to do wtih the dataset itself, because this can perform well on others datasets, and I also use part of  MS-Celeb-1M to change the number of the classes and samples of the dataset, but the result is the same.

issue_title: [SPARSE] undeterministic result of sparse dot(csr, dense, transpose_a=True)
issue_body:
The following test failed because of the limited precision of fp32 and the usage of atomicAdd in sparse dot implementation: 
```
    def check_dot_determinism(lhs_stype, rhs_stype, lhs_density, rhs_density, transpose_a, transpose_b):
        lhs_shape = (2, 200)
        rhs_shape = (2, 200)
        lhs = rand_ndarray(lhs_shape, lhs_stype, density=lhs_density)
        rhs = rand_ndarray(rhs_shape, rhs_stype, density=rhs_density)
        res1 = mx.nd.sparse.dot(lhs, rhs, transpose_a=transpose_a, transpose_b=transpose_b)
        res2 = mx.nd.sparse.dot(lhs, rhs, transpose_a=transpose_a, transpose_b=transpose_b)

        assert_almost_equal(res1.asnumpy(), res2.asnumpy(), rtol=0.0, atol=0.0)
    check_dot_determinism('csr', 'default', 0.5, 1.0, True, False)
```

issue_title: Mxnet R Package Installation Doc Bug
issue_body:
Wrong:

```
# current repo doc:  https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/tree/master/R-package
cran <- getOption("repos")
cran["dmlc"] <- "https://apache-mxnet.s3-accelerate.dualstack.amazonaws.com/R/CRAN/"
options(repos = cran)
install.packages("mxnet",dependencies = T)
```

Correct:
```
cran <- getOption("repos")
cran["dmlc"] <- "https://s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com/apache-mxnet/R/CRAN/"
options(repos = cran)
install.packages("mxnet",dependencies = T)
```

## Reference

https://stackoverflow.com/questions/43872455/mxnet-package-installation-in-r

issue_title: py_zipkin doesn't work correctly in threaded applications
issue_body:
Something like this:
````python
from py_zipkin.zipkin import zipkin_span

def main():
    with zipkin_span(service_name='main', span_name='kicking_off_threads'):
        threads = []
        for i in range(5):
            t = threading.Thread(target=self.test_threading)
            threads.append(t)
            t.start()

@zipkin_span(service_name='test_threading', span_name='test_threading')
def test_threading():
    print 'in test_threading'
    return
````

In this example, only the kicking_off_threads span is logged. This is due to the fact that the zipkin context is stored in the local thread, so other threads have no knowledge of the ongoing trace. Can we/should we support this use case?

issue_title: Do not include "typing" lib in `install_requires` if Python 3.5+
issue_body:
From https://pypi.python.org/pypi/typing:
> NOTE: in Python 3.5 and later, the typing module lives in the stdlib, and installing this package has NO EFFECT.

We should not force the install of `typing` for Python version 3.5 or later.


I'm suggesting the following change:

```
install_requires = ['six>=1.2']
if sys.version_info < (3, 5):
    install_requires.append('typing')

setup(
    [....]
    install_requires=install_requires,
    [....]
)
```

@trbs let me know what you think.

issue_title: Wrong description in ssh tunnel
issue_body:
Hi,

I noticed a wrong description in zmq/sss/tunnel.py here:
```
def` paramiko_tunnel( ... ):

    keyfile : str; path to public key file
        This specifies a key to be used in ssh login, default None.
        Regular default ssh keys will be used without specifying this argument.
```
and here:
```
def openssh_tunnel( .. ):

    keyfile : str; path to public key file
        This specifies a key to be used in ssh login, default None.
        Regular default ssh keys will be used without specifying this argument.
```
the keyfile is used as an '-i' argument for ssh command
```
    ssh="ssh "
    if keyfile:
        ssh += "-i " + keyfile
```
which according to ssh documentation should be a PRIVATE key, not PUBLIC
```
-i identity_file
Selects a file from which the identity (private key) for public key authentication is read.
```

Thanks!

issue_title: Tornado Context keeps old IOLoop
issue_body: The Tornado `Context` class stored the IOLoop current instance at the time of instantiation, and then forces all sockets to use that loop. This breaks any use where the current loop is closed and replaced with another one (it's common to have a distinct IO loop per unit test, for example).

issue_title: No network-based error on session.run()
issue_body:
I'm using the driver version 1.0.0, installed from pip on Ubuntu 15.10.
I want the session to be open for more than mere seconds, thus I'm concerned about a specific case where the database is live during the
 ```        
         try:
            session = driver.session()
        except ProtocolError:
            print("Cannot connect to neo4j. Aborting.")
```

but not during the subsequent `session.run(string)` with string as the formatted CYPHER request.
Thus, I would like to make sure that I detect any session-level error, and exit or try to fix the issue depending of its nature.

By manually starting the database, executing the `self.session = driver.session()`, then shutting down the database and calling the `session.run(string)` does not yield any error.

However, `ProtocolError` is thrown on `session.close()`.

It doesn't seem logic for those two network-based functions to have `ProtocolError` thrown and not for `session.run`.

With this current system, I cannot tell if a communication error appeared until I choose to close the session. As I want the session open for quite a long time, opening and closing the session for each run doesn't seem like a very good option.

issue_title: Make iterating over all entries default
issue_body:
Current API behavior is to return only the first page of results (defaults to first 50 entries) when querying a set, for examples files or tasks.

```
# iterates only over first 50 files in project
for f in api.files.query(project="myprojecid"):
    print(f.name)
```

If one wants to iterate over all result entries, the `.all()` notation must be used:

```
# iterates over all files in project
for f in api.files.query(project="myprojecid").all():
    print(f.name)
```

I would say this is currently _the_ most overlooked issue for API novices. Personally, this bit me several times when I started using the API, with typically 20+ minutes debugging time because the problem does not manifest itself in any output or error log. I have also seen several other users struggling with this when they got to learn the API. In addition, this error typically shows after deployment, because developers tend to work on small sets < 50 entries. 

I know there are very good technical reasons for this behavior. But if pagination could somehow happen silently in the background without the user noticing, that would be preferred.

issue_title: Return non-zero exit code on task failure
issue_body:
Is there a way to get the luigi CLI to return non-zero exit codes on task failures? It seems by default it only prints the task status in human readable form to stderr. We would like our scripts to know if the tasks succeeded, luigi returns 0 even if tasks have failed.

To work around this we have created a wrapper script as follows:

```bash
# get the luigi results from stderr
results="$(luigi "${@:1}" 2>&1)"

# print the results for debugging
echo "$results"

# grep the results to return a proper exit code
echo "$results" | grep 'This progress looks :)'
```

Is there a better way? Or would you be open to a PR to add this functionality?

issue_title: Support for Mediaflux S3 in Client
issue_body:
I'm working on a project backed by a non-AWS S3 installation.  It uses Mediaflux S3 (http://www.arcitecta.com/Products/Mediaflux).  That was not my decision, and I know this means AWS isnt getting paid ;-), but I'd like to still use boto3 as my client for this.   Mediaflux is an Australian product and it likes to use commas in float values i.e "2345,67" instead of "2345.67" in its ContentLength headers.  This causes a fatal error in boto3 and makes it impossible to use boto3 against Mediaflux.  To resolve this, I successfully monkey-patched botocore.

Gist:
https://gist.github.com/sachsbl/f6619b1d735641233e6e6b68fbb5aa58

Wrapped-client implementation:
https://github.com/sachsbl/Mediaflux_S3

I would like to apply this patch to botocore itself so we can abandon my monkey-patch approach.  I plan on opening a Pull Request with the small change required.  Thanks!

issue_title: Custom firefox profile doesn't work in Windows
issue_body:
[Documentation](http://splinter.readthedocs.org/en/latest/drivers/firefox.html#how-to-use-a-specific-profile-for-firefox) says you can select an existing firefox profile instead of creating a temporary one each time:

```
browser = Browser('firefox', profile='my_profile')
```

But this doesn't actually work, it doesn't know where to search for the profile:

```
browser = Browser('firefox', profile='default')
...
WindowsError: [Error 3] The system cannot find the path specified: 'default/*.*'
```

same for `profile='272kp1a1.default'`, `profile='272kp1a1'`, etc. though it isn't explained in the docs which type of string is correct.

issue_title: extractWithoutOrder
issue_body:
Hello
I was using the version 0.11.1 and the method process.extractWithoutOrder with no problem but I guess there was a change in the method on version 0.15.0. Can you please confirm it?

Using the code:
```
site_store_min_name = processor_name(site_store)
r = list(process.extractWithoutOrder(site_store_min_name, all_stores, processor=processor_name, scorer=fuzz.token_sort_ratio))
```
processor_name is - lambda y: y['min_name']
and all_stores is an array of dicts each with the property 'min_name'.

On version 0.11.1 I had to call the processor_name on the dict object 'site_store' before calling extractWithoutOrder with the first parameter 'site_store_min_name'.
On version 0.15.0 that would generate an error because extractWithoutOrder will try to call processor_name with a string as parameter.

Changing the code to:
```
# site_store_min_name = processor_name(site_store)
r = list(process.extractWithoutOrder(site_store, all_stores, processor=processor_name, scorer=fuzz.token_sort_ratio))
```
seems to work. Is this expected?

issue_body:
Hello all,
we are currently trying to get CSE running but we are stumpling over some security concerns.
It looks like from the configuration file and its script (config.py) that the vCenter user name is not taken from the configuration but instead it's always the administrator@vsphere.local account.
Is there a reason for it or would it be possible to correct the statement in the config.py file?
e.g. old:
`v = VSphere(vsphere_url.hostname, vcenter.Username.text, vc['password'], vsphere_url.port)`
new:
`v = VSphere(vsphere_url.hostname, vc['username'], vc['password'], vsphere_url.port)`

Regards
Markus

issue_title: Allow passing query parameters as a params kwarg to underlying Requests
issue_body:
It would be nice if the query params could be specified as a kwarg to the `get`, `post`, et al methods as the [underlying Requests library does](http://docs.python-requests.org/en/master/user/quickstart/#passing-parameters-in-urls). 

Ex:
```python
wcapi = API(...)
params = {
    'status': 'cancelled',
    'per_page': 25,
    'page': 1
}
r = wcapi.get('orders', params=params)
```

issue_title: [Windows] Unable to add files to index if corresponding object file is read-only
issue_body:
Seen on 
`OS`: Windows 7
`git.__version__`: 1.0.1
`python --version`: Python 3.4.3

#### Description
If an object file becomes read-only, trying to add the corresponding working directory file to an index causes a File Access Error:

Running the program below following with the object file for 'test.txt' set to read-only triggers the error (see Traceback at bottom).
This appears to be because the store() function of the underlying loose DB tries to remove the file before renaming a temp file onto it. However, on windows, read-only files cannot be removed with os.remove - one must remove the read only attribute first. 

Although this might seem like a farfetched scenario, in reality I've hit this in several real usage scenarios (though I have not been able to characterize exactly why an object file becomes read-only). So, I think this is generally valid (and indeed this would work fine on *nix due to the fact that read-only files can still be deleted with os.remove)

I do not know enough about the way GIT stores objects to say what the best solution would be here, but one option would be to check for this case and explicilty remove the read-only attribute before attempting to remove.

#### Program

    import git
    repo_path = r"C:\Users\ach\Documents\code\odds_ends\gptest"
    broken_path = r"C:\Users\ach\Documents\code\odds_ends\gptest\test.txt"
    repo = git.Repo(repo_path)
    idx = repo.index
    idx.add(items=[broken_path])

#### Traceback

issue_title: Commit without executing hooks
issue_body:
If I do `index.commit(msg)` it will execute [`pre-commit`](https://github.com/gitpython-developers/GitPython/blob/d5739cd466f77a60425bd2860895799f7c9359d9/git/index/base.py#L941) and [`post-commit`](https://github.com/gitpython-developers/GitPython/blob/d5739cd466f77a60425bd2860895799f7c9359d9/git/index/base.py#L946)

issue_title: TextFields with "onChange" js-event needs to be filled twice to values be available
issue_body:
Environment: Wicket-based form, which validates text inputs
Problem: When "Input text" is ran first time, it empties the field (and value attribute), and causes event to fire (at this case submitting the form). When (in same command from Robot Framework) command tries to add text, it can't find element anymore. 

Workaround is to make Input text first inside "Run keyword and ignore error", but this just seems quite stupid way (plus it generates extra screenshot/page source in log).

Second time the event isn't fired, which causes need for following action to be performed twice (first launches the event, and second time puts the correct value to dropdown's selected).

issue_title: Compatibility with existing color forumulas
issue_body:
The color formulas for each source are defined in a `color` file containing the command line arguments to imagemagick `convert`

We need to translate these to rio color arguments. Two main options

1. ~~Have a `rio color --compatability` option that will read convert args and interpret them on the fly~~
2. Script/manually adjust all the color formulas in pxm-sources. Create an alternate `color.rio` file that mirrors the intent of `color` but with `rio color` syntax

Questions:
* what do we do with convert options that don't translate well to rio color?
* how are we going to visually test to make sure rio color output matches convert's?

issue_title: (IGRA) Feature to provide information on the number of soundings for a given day and their launch hour
issue_body:
My biggest hurdle while going through the IGRA data is that different stations have different sounding hours. The most general is probably the 00 or 12 UTC but many stations also have soundings at odd times.  For eg,
station id UKM00003026 has 3 soundings on 31 Dec 1997 at 5, 11 and 23 UTC. As of now, the data can be only imported if the launching hours on a particular date is known beforehand.
Hence, I request for a way to check the sounding hours of a particular data while trying to import the data. 

What I tried in the function I wrote for reading IGRA data is to just print all the data for a given day and thus get an idea of the sounding time. It's not the prettiest approach but something along the lines of returning all the launch hours for a given date from the headers would be nice.

Thanks,

issue_title: Add default filename for download
issue_labels: [{'id': 246269918, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWwyNDYyNjk5MTg=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/Unidata/siphon/labels/Area:%20Catalog', 'name': 'Area: Catalog', 'color': 'c7def8', 'default': False}, {'id': 230408999, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWwyMzA0MDg5OTk=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/Unidata/siphon/labels/Type:%20Enhancement', 'name': 'Type: Enhancement', 'color': 'fad8c7', 'default': False}, {'id': 908908783, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw5MDg5MDg3ODM=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/Unidata/siphon/labels/good%20first%20issue', 'name': 'good first issue', 'color': 'd4c5f9', 'default': True}]
issue_body:
Right now, `Dataset.download()` requires a filename to be passed in. We should make that optional, defaulting to the value of `Dataset.name`. So download should look roughly like
```python
    def download(self, filename=None):
        if filename is None:
            filename = self.name
        with self.remote_open() as infile:
            with open(filename, 'wb') as outfile:
                outfile.write(infile.read())
```
with of course an updated docstring.

issue_title: Add more tests for PEP 517
issue_body: One issue with [this code](https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/blob/b729553a6c6b92dfc7cfd9c577f2399bfde3000e/setuptools/build_meta.py#L120-L124) is that it's completely untested. In some cases, setup.py egg_info can create a directory not in the `egg_base`, but in a subdirectory of the egg base. We need to figure out when this can happen, and then add a test for it.

issue_title: Make pheno-vs-pheno comparisons
issue_body:
On the pheno page, add a button "compare to other phenotype".  When clicked, show an autocomplete searchbox of phenotype names.  When one is selected, show:

1. Both Manhattan plots, shorter.  For traits that have a p-value better than 1e-40, it would really help to have the graph fold at 1e-10 (or wherever) so that other significant peaks are still visible.

2. A neglog10pval-vs-neglog10pval plot.  The Manhattan data only contains the 2000 most-significant associations, so variants binned in both datasets would be left out, variants binned in one would be on an axis, and the rare few variants binned in neither would be out in plot.  To make this more useful, I'd like to have the p-value in both datasets for every variant in the top 2000 of either phenotype.  So, build an API `/api/variants-lookup/` where I can POST a JSONified list of 2000 `{chrom}-{pos}-{ref}-{alt}`s?

3. A table of variants with pval < 5e-8 in both phenotypes?  What's the right way to compute this within a dataset?  What's the right way to compute this on-the-fly for phenotypes in different datasets?  First, store a list of variants with pval<5e-8 for each phenotype, sorted by chrom-pos.  Then offer `/api/phenotype-intersection/<pheno1>/<pheno2>`?

issue_title: YAML 1.2 spec: Example 8.2. Block Indentation Indicator
issue_body:
When I try to parse example 8.2 at http://www.yaml.org/spec/1.2/spec.html#Block
I got:
```
[ 'detected\n',
  '\n\n# detected\n',
  '\n explicit\n',
  '\n\t\ndetected\n' ]
```
But according to the canonical form last two lines must not get a leading '\n':
```
%YAML 1.2
---
!!seq [
  !!str "detected\n",
  !!str "\n\n# detected\n",
  !!str "·explicit\n",
  !!str "\t·detected\n",
]
```

issue_title: Support DELETE on Integrations
issue_body:
In PagerDuty support case #85888, I received confirmation that the v2 API does, in fact, support the deletion of individual integrations (on a service).  The `Integration` class in this library rejects deletes.  (The `delete` and `remove` methods are explicitly overridden to raise `InvalidEndpoint` and `InvalidEndpointOperation` exceptions, respectively.)  That's one problem.

The second problem involves the resource paths for integrations.  These paths are nested beneath their parent services.  e.g.: `/services/SERVICE-ID/integrations/INTEGRATION-ID`.  The default `Entity` endpoint resolution code does not work in this case.  Even if the overridden `delete` and `remove` methods were to be removed, resource deletion would fail because the requests would be addressed to `/integrations/INTEGRATION-ID` (missing leading `/services/SERVICE-ID`).  I'm not sure how best to solve this problem.   Endpoints seem to get resolved at the class layer when what (I think) we need here is resolution at the instance layer.

Thoughts?

issue_title: Thread leakages in simple consumer and producer
issue_body:
I have a program which creates one of my own threads when a certain call happens. That thread of mine creates a simpleconsumer.

The def run() of my thread does something like this:

```
	topic = kafka_client.topics['test_topic']
	consumer = topic.get_simple_consumer( 'group_id' )
```

Note that `kafka_client` is global -- outside of my thread. That is, there is only one of them for all threads. But, consumer is local: there's one per thread.  When my thread's run() stops cleanly it calls:

```
	consumer.stop()
```

Yet when I look at the thread count I see that it grows by one every time I start and stop one of my threads.  If I take out the call to get the consumer then it doesn't. It appears as though there is a thread leak in the simple_consumer.

issue_title: Documentation around refund_application_fee unclear
issue_body:
I have an application that charges a fee on transactions. I can use that application to fetch (and I believe refund) charges during the operation of my application. But if I add refund_application_fee=True to my call, I get the following error:

```
InvalidRequestError: Only an application generating a fee can call refund_application_fee on <charge_id>
```

This is because I need to use the access token associated with the connected account, NOT my application's token. This was unclear to me from the docs and from this error message.

My application *is* in a certain sense "generating" the fee. I'm the cause of it. What I need to do is run the call as the *owner of the charge*. Now that I know that, it totally makes sense, but it was very confusing for a while.

issue_title: Numerical inaccuracies with the random_walker
issue_body:
## Description
The random walker segmentation code is very useful but behaves sometimes very strangely with respect to numerical results.

See following code (sorry this is slow):

result:

Should be between 0 and 1: [0.000000 ; 1.103701]

## Way to reproduce

import numpy as np
from skimage.filters import gaussian
from skimage.segmentation import random_walker


np.random.seed(42)
blob3d = np.random.randn(101,101,101) # 3D image of random noise
blob3ds = gaussian(blob3d,sigma=10.) # makes a Gaussian random field
p05=np.percentile(blob3ds,5.) # Normalize the GRF between 0 and 1
p95=np.percentile(blob3ds,95.)
blob3ds = (blob3ds-p05)/(p95-p05)
blob3ds = np.clip(blob3ds,0,1)

markers3d = np.zeros((101,101,101)) 
markers3d[45:55,45:55,45:55] = 1  # central box
markers3d[:,:,0]=markers3d[:,0,:]=markers3d[0,:,:] = 2 # border of the box
markers3d[100,:,:]=markers3d[:,100,:] = markers3d[:,:,100] = 2 # border of the box cont.

grow3d = random_walker(blob3ds, markers3d, beta=5e4, mode='mg_cg', tol=1e-5, return_full_prob=True)

print "Should be between 0 and 1: [%f ; %f]" % (np.min(grow3d[1]), np.max(grow3d[1]))

issue_title: Bug in feature.peak_local_max leading to IndexError for ND arrays, N != 2
issue_body:
Hi,

There is a bug there:

https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/blob/master/skimage/feature/peak.py#L152

this line assumes that we are working with 2D arrays while the rest of the code is for ND arrays.

I propose the following fix:

    intensities = image.flat[np.ravel_multi_index(coordinates,image.shape)]

I usually follow the rule "if it's not broken, don't fix it", so you can ignore the following suggestion:

https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/blob/master/skimage/feature/peak.py#L149

    coordinates = np.transpose((image > peak_threshold).nonzero())

could be written:

    coordinates = np.argwhere(image > peak_threshold)

See http://docs.scipy.org/doc/numpy/reference/generated/numpy.argwhere.html

"np.argwhere(a) is the same as np.transpose(np.nonzero(a))."

Kind regards,

Kevin

issue_title: HOG kwarg 'normalise' does not normalize data, replace it
issue_body:
When `normalise=True`, HOG operates on the square root of the raw input data. See [this line](https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/blob/master/skimage/feature/_hog.py#L70).

This is a problem because `np.sqrt` is not a normalization. It is a transform of the data. The data is not scaled or guaranteed to have a particular range after it is applied; it's just a square root. What's more, the square root is not defined for all input data.

As a result, users will apply this kwarg thinking it will normalize their data when in fact it may be creating problems (see #1889).

Generally, kwargs should have intuitive names. This one is bad enough I think it should be loudly deprecated in advance of 0.12. 

An acceptable resolution would include:

* Replace `normalise` with something like `transform_sqrt`.
* If this code path is used, check the minimum value of the input array. If it is negative, warn the user their result will have NaNs.

issue_title: Windows Python support
issue_body:
The relocation of the [SWIG](http://www.swig.org/) wrapper and bindings from NuPIC into this core library, changes the porting tasks involved with getting all of NuPIC working on Windows OS. 

The following two goals still need to be achieved -

1. Automated builds (of Python v2.7 only) can download, use and bind to appropriate versions of the core library.
2. The Windows nupic.core deployment package is compatible with Windows NuPIC (64 bit Python2_7.exe).

Many have contributed to how to achieve this  [...]. With success mainly affected by the level of C++ language features required at the time. NuPIC Python and the Core library were built and working (up to tests requiring database access) with 32 bit Python 2.7.x late in 2014 [...]. Since then C++11 requirements have affected the MSVC compiler that can be used for building the core. The current AppVeyor build [...] is stalled due to the need of an old Window's SDK (pre-Vista version?). Python 3.5 sees support for Visual Studio 2015 [...]. Supplying a route that could solve this issue. Potential routes towards solving this issue are;

1. The core library built and binding within NuPIC (current work)
2. Python 3.5+ only support for Windows (guaranteed solution)
3. Python 3.5+ for Windows, with 2.8 support for Linux incl. OS X (2to3 track)
4. Rebuild everything with Visual Studio 2015 (Python exe and libs, NumPy and Core libs, etc., big dependency filled package)
5. An application that embeds Python (mimics language interpreter; dynamic Python and static core linkage)

Some of these routes feel like dead ends. Such as [1 and 4 ...]. Others look fraught with problems [clang via MinGW GCC, 5 ...]. **TODO** - Break each down. Add the pros and cons for each. Add source reference links. Format this super-issue and arrange sub-issues. Labels, Milestone, Assignees, Feedback, Guidelines and Markdown.

issue_title: Add a settings property on backends
issue_body:
When a backend is instanciated, it gets some settings and creates a connection object with it. In some our tests we're checking the values of those settings to check that they are correctly passed along.

I propose a simple standard for all our backends to expose those settings: a "settings" read-only property that will let us read the values.

Implementation example for the redis backends:

```python

class RedisBackend:
    @property
    def settings(self):
        data = dict(self._client.connection_pool.connection_kwargs)
        data['max_connections'] = self._client.connection_pool.max_connections
        return data
```

via `version` argument:

```
/record/18/files/thesis.pdf?version=2
```

It would be useful to have a unique way of referring to past versions
of resources handled by the digital library, be they records or
documents (or other related information like discussion).

This could be done by introducing a new `wayback` URL argument that
would bring, as best as possible, the past version of a resource:

```
/record/18/export/xm?wayback=YYYYMMDDhhmmss
/record/18/files/thesis.pdf?wayback=YYYYMMDDhhmmss
```

The URLs would be internally translated to ones using `revision` (for
record metadata) and `version` (for file) and corresponding functions
would be called before serving the results.  So the `wayback` layer
could be relatively thin.

However, it may be suitable to extend this to other resources, such as
HTML detailed formats or XML reference list or even discussion
threads:

```
/record/18/?wayback=YYYYMMDDhhmmss
/record/18/references?wayback=YYYYMMDDhhmmss
/record/18/discussion?wayback=YYYYMMDDhhmmss
```

One possibility would be to create a new wayback module with a
waybackify daemon that would run in the background and go through
resources decorated with `@waybackify` (or defined in some
configuration variable) and take historical snapshots of results into
history store.  The serving part would then look in this wayback store
and serve what is available for given time snapshot.

(This would be useful for future Memento RFC 7089 support.)

issue_title: Android Dropdown and Windows disable multitouch.
issue_body:
```
from kivy.config import Config
# The 'Config.set()' below disposes of nasty red dot on Windows on two finger tap
# (the recommended workaround for disabling multitouch emulation)
# But makes Dropdown contents unresponsive on Android
# In this example, on Android but not Windows:
# Tap the "hello' button and the dropdown appears.
# Tap anywhere and the dropdown does not respond, a case is never selected, 
# or the dropdown dismissed

Config.set('input', 'mouse', 'mouse,multitouch_on_demand')


### The remainder of this file is Dropdown 'Basic Example' copied from:
# https://kivy.org/docs/api-kivy.uix.dropdown.html

from kivy.uix.dropdown import DropDown
from kivy.uix.button import Button
from kivy.base import runTouchApp

# create a dropdown with 10 buttons
dropdown = DropDown()
for index in range(10):
    # When adding widgets, we need to specify the height manually
    # (disabling the size_hint_y) so the dropdown can calculate
    # the area it needs.

    btn = Button(text='Value %d' % index, size_hint_y=None, height=44)

    # for each button, attach a callback that will call the select() method
    # on the dropdown. We'll pass the text of the button as the data of the
    # selection.
    btn.bind(on_release=lambda btn: dropdown.select(btn.text))

    # then add the button inside the dropdown
    dropdown.add_widget(btn)

# create a big main button
mainbutton = Button(text='Hello', size_hint=(None, None))

# show the dropdown menu when the main button is released
# note: all the bind() calls pass the instance of the caller (here, the
# mainbutton instance) as the first argument of the callback (here,
# dropdown.open.).
mainbutton.bind(on_release=dropdown.open)

# one last thing, listen for the selection in the dropdown list and
# assign the data to the button text.
dropdown.bind(on_select=lambda instance, x: setattr(mainbutton, 'text', x))

runTouchApp(mainbutton)

```

A common workaround for a Red dot that, on Windows, Kivy paints due a two finger tap is to disable multitouch emulation. On Android this disables Dropdown functionality. 

The real problem is Kivy's Red dot. The workaround is simple. But the cause was not intuitive (to me), so please consider trapping this and turning it off.

issue_title: API: Yahoo option type labels
issue_body:
#244 changed how option types are described from put and call to puts and calls.

This is a breaking API change and is different than how the google option reader describes them.
Docs broken [here](https://pandas-datareader.readthedocs.io/en/latest/remote_data.html#yahoo-finance-options)

Any objection to changing this back to put and call? Semantically, as the index is referring to an individual option, I think the singular is more appropriate.


Example:
```
In [1]: 
from pandas_datareader import Options
tsla = Options('tsla', 'yahoo')
data = tsla.get_all_data()
data.index.levels[2]

Out[1]: 
Index(['calls', 'puts'], dtype='object', name='Type')

In [2]:
tsla = Options('tsla', 'google')
data = tsla.get_options_data(expiry=tsla.expiry_dates[0])
data.index.levels[2]
Out[2]:
Index(['call', 'put'], dtype='object', name='Type')

```

issue_title: Investigate possible bugged holdings plots
issue_body: Some of the holdings plots may be incorrectly displaying positions. This should be investigated — the plots should be checked against the holdings data.

issue_title: Standardize style (axis, legend, labels, capitalization, ...) across all plots
issue_body:
Currently, pyfolio is a jumble of styles. Different plots have different looking legends, some plots don't have their labels capitalized, etc. Some differing style is obviously fine, but we should adopt a basic standard that is used in new plots.

I don't think this will too much effort — the bulk is just standardizing the legends, I believe. I'm not sure if breaking out some `style_plot` function is the answer, because there will be small differences between plots.

Things I can think of:
- Capitalize plot titles/legends/axis labels correctly
- Ensure proper rotation of axis labels
- Standardize legend style (I'm personally a fan of the transparent background-box)
- Standardize font sizes (might already be done)

issue_title: New release for pyfolio
issue_body:
It looks like [enough has changed](https://github.com/quantopian/pyfolio/compare/v0.7.0...master) on pyfolio to merit v0.8.0 (with risk and performance attribution, I think we might even want to call it v1.0.0!)

We are also seeing an increasing number of users running into bugs that have already been patched: upping the version would make it easier to diagnose errors (both ours, and open source users).

Major highlights:
- Risk tear sheet #391 
- Simple tear sheet #389 
- Rolling annual volatility plot #390 
- Yahoo + pandas data reader bug fix #395 
- `information_ratio` [compatibility with empyrical](https://github.com/quantopian/pyfolio/commit/a34222e6a065b85147e9e7e53cce4549058bd51e) 

To-do:
- [ ] Performance attribution tear sheet #400 (still WIP, will definitely want to wait for this)
- [ ] Example notebooks for documentation #411 (ready for review and merge)
- [ ] Fama-French multivariate regression bug fix #406 (fairly serious bug fix, but Travis is sad)
- [ ] Resolve any dependency issues with zipline/alphalens/empyrical
- [ ] Do we want to outsource Fama-French functionality from pyfolio to empyrical? [#56 on empyrical](https://github.com/quantopian/empyrical/pull/56)
- [ ] Do we want to include returns decomposition from alphalens into pyfolio? [#183 on alphalens](https://github.com/quantopian/alphalens/pull/183)

@twiecki @gusgordon @richafrank @Jstauth thoughts? If this sounds good I'll organize a milestone :)

issue_title: PySpark support
issue_body:
I'm looking to integrate Sentry into a PySpark project.  Behind the scenes PySpark wraps and runs the Scala Spark code on the JVM.  This makes exception aggregation a bit more complicated vs pure Python code.

The Scala spark code uses log4j which I noticed Sentry's Java client supports.

I'm wondering if it'd be possible to use Sentry, the Python client, Java client, or something else to aggregate exceptions on PySpark code?

issue_body:
- [ ] Publish `datatable` wheels into proper bucket on S3: `s3://h2o-release/datatable/`. Currently we place datatable builds into `s3://artifacts.h2o.ai/releases/ai/h2o/pydatatable/` which is very hard to find, and gives wrong impression that datatable is not a standalone project.
- [ ] Decide whether we want to publish after each commit to master, or on a nightly basis.
- [ ] Create redirect files to the "latest nightly" and "latest stable" wheels. There should be separate files for different flavors of wheels: linux/macosx/ppc/centos/linux-debug/macos-debug. Redirect should be "hard", so that following the link would immediately initiate download of the relevant wheel.
- [ ] Add links to those redirect files in the README.md front page.

issue_title: Give hints about possibly misspelled column names
issue_body:
This is similar to https://github.com/Rdatatable/data.table/issues/2887

It is rather common for a user to misspell their column names. Currently the error message says the following:

>>> d0 = dt.Frame(foo=[1], bar=[2], baz=[3])

ValueError: Column `bae` does not exist in Frame

ValueError: Column `bae` does not exist in Frame; did you mean `bar` or `baz`?

issue_title: Support Unpack Generalizations on slicing
issue_body:
Python 3.5 genarilized unpacking to support more than one on function calls:
```python
def f(i, j, k, l, **kwargs):
    pass
a = [1, 2]
b = [3, 4]
c = {'x': 5}
d = {'y': 6}

f(*a, *b, **c, **d)
```
We should create dependencies:
i -> a
j -> a
k -> b
l -> b
kwargs -> c, d

issue_title: All action commands should take object lookup params like --name
issue_body:
This can't be done in this format:

```
$ tower-cli inventory_source update --name=my_source
Error: no such option: --name
```

This is non-standard behavior. Instead, we should use fields as options, perform the object lookup, and then proceede with the update.

issue_title: 清空 /tmp/.qiniu_pythonsdk_hostscache.json 导致异常
Consider using better health checking on Zookeeper and Consul.
issue_body:
Zookeeper has ephemeral nodes, and Consul has health checks.  Either of these mechanisms provides a means to detect master failure -- and even replica failure -- faster than via the polling method we need to use with etcd.

We should consider using this advanced mechanism on each of these SIS platforms.

issue_title: Do not install typing when running on the latest Python version
issue_body:
### Description

Connexion has a hard dependency on `typing` which is useless when running the latest version of Python.


### Expected behaviour

Installing Connexion does not install typing when the interpreter is Python 3.6.1.

### Actual behaviour

```
$ pip install connexion
...
Collecting typing>=3.6.1 (from connexion)
  Downloading typing-3.6.1.tar.gz (66kB)
...
```

### Steps to reproduce

Install Connexion with Python 3.6.1.

### Additional info:

Python 3.6.1 on MacOS
Connexion Version: 1.1.10

issue_title: Unset COLORTERM
issue_body:
`COLORTERM` is an environment variable set by some terminal emulators such as `gnome-terminal`.
To avoid confusing applications running inside Guake it would be better to clean up `COLORTERM` (or set it to a custom value).

issue_title: `Expected URI`s in testdata don't match with the real one
issue_body:
When I run the tests in Travis-CI and in my computer. Some of the tests are failing because of the expected result is not same as the real one.

One of them is https://travis-ci.org/refeed/py-memento-client/jobs/253494646#L391
```
[gw23] linux -- Python 3.5.3 /home/travis/virtualenv/python3.5.3/bin/python
input_uri_r = 'http://www.cnn.com'
input_datetime = datetime.datetime(1998, 7, 28, 17, 18, 49)
input_timegate = 'http://web.archive.org/web/'
expected_uri_m = 'http://web.archive.org/web/20000815052826/http://www.cnn.com/'
    @pytest.mark.parametrize("input_uri_r,input_datetime,input_timegate,expected_uri_m", specified_timegate_testdata)
    def test_get_memento_uri_specified_timegate(input_uri_r, input_datetime, input_timegate, expected_uri_m):
    
        mc = MementoClient(timegate_uri=input_timegate, check_native_timegate=False)
    
        actual_uri_m = mc.get_memento_info(input_uri_r, input_datetime).get("mementos").get("closest").get("uri")[0]
    
>       assert expected_uri_m == actual_uri_m
E       AssertionError: assert 'http://web.a.../www.cnn.com/' == 'http://web.ar...w.cnn.com:80/'
E         Skipping 50 identical leading characters in diff, use -v to show
E         - ww.cnn.com/
E         + ww.cnn.com:80/
E         ?           +++
test/test_memento_client.py:93: AssertionError
```
The `AssertionError` above is happen because the real one which has `:80` on it (port) is not same as the expected one which doesn't have the port. The port above is added from the timegate server itself which is web.archive.org.

This is the result when I do a `curl` for url http://web.archive.org/web/20000815052826/http://www.cnn.com/ :
```
$ curl --head "http://web.archive.org/web/20000815052826/http://www.cnn.com/"
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Server: Tengine/2.1.0
Date: Fri, 14 Jul 2017 12:06:49 GMT
Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8
Content-Length: 79991
Connection: keep-alive
X-Archive-Orig-date: Tue, 15 Aug 2000 05:28:24 GMT
X-Archive-Orig-set-cookie: CNNid=cf19470d-11709-966317304-1; expires=Wednesday, 30-Dec-2037 16:00:00 GMT; path=/; domain=.cnn.com

X-ts: ----

X-location: All
X-Page-Cache: MISS

As we can see above, `web.archive.org` is adding the port number on the last of the url which is different with the expected one.

So I think `web.archive.org` has been updated its headers content format. There are still many `AssertionError`s, which are still similar with this case, like `http://`s are changed to `https://`, etc.

issue_title: Feature request for some standard dimension-wise and broadcasting functions
issue_body:
Missing from Python:
* I see that a `SumDimension` node exists in C++, but `sum_dim` is missing in the Python interface. Would it be possible to add a wrapper? (This would also address the lack of a `sum_rows` operation analogous to the existing `sum_cols` operation.)

Nice to have:
* Would it be possible to add a dimension-wise logsumexp operation called `logsumexp_dim`? This would be useful when implementing a CRF, for example. We currently have `esum` and `logsumexp` for lists of expressions. If `sum_dim` mentioned above is added, users could implement `logsumexp_dim` in terms `sum_dim` and `max_dim`, but a built-in numerically stable version would be ideal.
* Would it be possible to add an addition operation that broadcasts over rows called `rowwise_add` analogous to the existing `colwise_add`? Or is there an alternative way to do broadcasting addition over rows using the current API other than transposing, performing `colwise_add`, and transposing back?

issue_title: What is and isn't saved with ParameterCollection.save()?
issue_body:
I am running into some strange behavior with saving and loading a model. Let's say I train my model for N epochs, updating some ParameterCollection object `pc`. At epoch M I get the best performance on some set, e.g. 60.1%. At each epoch I save the model using `pc.save("epoch-#.save")`. After training, I want to evaluate using the version of the model at epoch M, using `pc.populate("epoch-M.save")`.

If I don't terminate the script, i.e. `pc` has been the same object since the beginning of training, I get the exact same performance -- 60.1%. Loading works just fine.

If I terminate the script, i.e. construct `pc` from scratch (also means initializing the values), and then populate it using `pc.populate("epoch-M.save")`, suddenly my accuracy is 0%! And the values it predicts are basically nonsense. The same thing happens if I train a model for a few epochs and then try to load from `"epoch-M.save"` (the earlier save file).

I verified that all of the parameters in my model have associated lines in the save file, and that all of the parameters I use were constructed with `pc`. So none of the parameters that I explicitly declare are being missed when saving. I also looked at the values being loaded -- they seem to be loaded fine, no matter whether I start training from scratch or load the model without any training. I'm also using the latest version of Dynet.

Before I try to chop down my code into a minimal working example, is there any possibility that something in the model isn't being saved to the save file (but persists in the `pc` object)? 

FWIW, `pc` includes a few lookup parameters, some matrices, and a few LSTMs (each of which seems to be associated with three `Parameters` when I look at `pc.parameteres_list()`.

issue_title: Problem handling local structured property kind without keys
issue_body:
According to: https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/python/ndb/properties#structured
Local structured properties do not require keys and AppEngine appears to be fine with this.
Unfortunately, the gcloud-python library expects keys for every entity, even if they're local structured ones.
My work around is to update my entities in AppEngine to provide dummy keys, however, it would be nice if the library supported it natively.

gcloud/datastore/helpers.pyc in entity_from_protobuf(pb)
     46     :returns: The entity derived from the protobuf.
     47     """
---> 48     key = key_from_protobuf(pb.key)
     49     entity_props = {}
     50     exclude_from_indexes = []

gcloud/datastore/helpers.pyc in key_from_protobuf(pb)
    103         namespace = pb.partition_id.namespace
    104 
--> 105     return Key(*path_args, namespace=namespace, dataset_id=dataset_id)

gcloud/datastore/key.pyc in __init__(self, *path_args, **kwargs)
     74         # _flat_path, _parent, _namespace and _dataset_id must be set before
     75         # _combine_args() is called.
---> 76         self._path = self._combine_args()

gcloud/datastore/key.pyc in _combine_args(self)
     131         :raises: :class:`ValueError` if the parent key is not complete.
     132         """
--> 133         child_path = self._parse_path(self._flat_path)

gcloud/datastore/key.pyc in _parse_path(path_args)
      92         """
      93         if len(path_args) == 0:
---> 94             raise ValueError('Key path must not be empty.')
 
ValueError: Key path must not be empty.

issue_title: Can't receive messages larger than 4MiB
issue_body:
Cloud Pub/Sub has a maximum message size of around 10 MiB ([source](https://cloud.google.com/pubsub/quotas#other_limits)). gRPC has a default max message size of 4MiB, so there are valid messages that cannot be pulled by google-cloud-python.

These messages appear to be silently dropped, as the pull request generates `<_Rendezvous of RPC that terminated with (StatusCode.RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, Received message larger than max (8106187 vs. 4194304))>` but this is considered to be a transient error so the request is retried. When the next call to Pull() returns no messages, the application receives no messages. (behaviour may be different with production Pub/Sub servers, as I encountered this testing against an emulator)

This limit was lifted for the BigTable client in #2907, but this change didn't affect other clients. For PubSub, it can be fixed by setting the max message size options ([as done for BigTable](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/7fa089cb2b3a67de9fc454fb25c9c1e99760141e/bigtable/google/cloud/bigtable/client.py#L82)) in [make_gax_publisher_api / make_gax_subscriber_api](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/7fa089cb2b3a67de9fc454fb25c9c1e99760141e/pubsub/google/cloud/pubsub/_gax.py#L655).

issue_title: google-cloud-core incompatible with App Engine (py27)
issue_body:
The polling library in google-cloud-core contains an indirect native dependency (`ctypes`) via the `tenacity` library. This has the unfortunate consequence of causing import failures on App Engine because the GAE runtime restricts the use of native dependencies.

To clarify the dependency chain causing the problem:

| Project | Description | Relevant Import Site
|--------|------------|-------------
|`google-cloud-core`||[`core/google/api/core/future/polling.py`](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/0723a9cb802a08c828af83049c80b68036fcfc3c/core/google/api/core/future/polling.py#L23)
|[`tenacity`](https://github.com/jd/tenacity)|General-purpose retry library|[`monotonic` import](https://github.com/jd/tenacity/blob/319168f18ecb7d95ead91f3d7aaada23dabe1161/tenacity/__init__.py#L28)
|[monotonic](https://github.com/atdt/monotonic)|py27 backport of py33 `time.monotonic`|[`ctypes` import](https://github.com/atdt/monotonic/blob/5c0322dc559bf961f7e111d97cd3ed9ab5c1a73b/monotonic.py#L41)|

I couldn't think of a particularly easy fix.

One wacky idea that might work would be to write a monotonic-like function/class that faked monotonicity by storing some fixed number of recent calls to the non-monotonic function (e.g. `time.time()`). When a new call came in, it would call the time function and instead of returning that value straight away, it would calculate and return the max of the value and the cached previous calls (and, to be definitionally correct, add an epsilon). Given the constraints of tenacity, this might suffice but a simpler solution would obviously be preferred.

Thanks :)

issue_title: Pytest Targets that "Error" rather than "Failure" don't show up in the list of failed targets
issue_body:
When we have a pytest target that has an error, rather than a failure, the results log says

```
E <target>
```

rather than 

```
F <target>
```

Then when the pytest runner looks at the results log to compile the list of failed targets, it misses the errors, because of this:

```
RESULTLOG_FAILED_PATTERN = re.compile(r'F (.?):.+)')
```

issue_title: Add --with-dependees option to pants idea and/or the plugin
issue_body:
We have a common scenario where folks want to refactor a library across the repo.

In this case, you want to launch the IDE with a particular library and all code that depend on it.

We have a recipe documented at Square for this right now which essentially is:

```
 ./pants idea <targets-to-load-in-ide>  `./pants dependees <target-to-refactor>`
```

It would be easier and more discoverable if you could just tell goal idea or the IntelliJ plugin to just load up all dependees of the targets you've specified.

issue_title: Task._should_cache_target_dirs returns a cache instance instead of True
issue_body:
The bug is not exactly in Task, but it is the main consumer. That method call returns an Artifact Cache object, a la `<pants.cache.local_artifact_cache.LocalArtifactCache object at 0x111219950>`.

All the methods names and docstrings promise a bool, but those `and` statements transitively terminate in an ArtifactCache factory. If you trace the call, you end up in the [cache_setup.read_cache_available() method](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/cache/cache_setup.py#L132).

That final call makes the `read_cache_available`, which one would expect to return boolean, instead return 3 totally different states:
- False if the options are disabled
- None if `get_read_cache()` finds no configured cache
- An ArtifactCache instance otherwise.

It is not clear to me that this behavior hasn't become relied upon in some subtle way so opening an issue for now.

issue_title: 1.0.0(-rc1) changelog not available on the docsite
issue_body:
The notes for 1.0.0 are not included on the docsite, because they were never existed in master. We should cherry-pick the notes to master as a one time fix.

But to prevent this issue in the future, we'll need to change the release process documentation to always require separate reviews for the notes and the version bump.

Proposal:
1. One review to merge notes to `master` (regardless of release branch)
2. A separate review to bump the version in the relevant branch (perhaps with a special case to allow for using just one review in the case of a snapshot bump)

issue_title: PythonChroot should guard against mismatched __init__.py
issue_body:
Right now, PythonChroot assembles user coded python_library targets as well as some python code-gen targets (thrift, antlr).  It does not currently check that shared package prefixes have compatible __init__.pys.  As a result, a chroot can successfully build, but fail to run.  Worse, the failures can be flaky depending on import order.  It would be better to detect incompatible __init__.pys of the same package path and fail the chroot freeze when these are detected with a helpful message.

See #1879 which was a confusing case that would have been clear if this fail-fast behavior existed.

This is not currently the case for Revision:
```
>>> Revision.semver('1.1.1') < Revision.semver('1.1.1-pre0')
True

issue_title: Adopt jarjar from googlecode into pantsbuild/jarjar
issue_body:
As we've done for ivy and jmake, but in this case the adoption motivation is perhaps more acute:
 1. Googlecode is going away 1/25/2016: http://google-opensource.blogspot.com/2015/03/farewell-to-google-code.html so [jarjar](https://code.google.com/p/jarjar/) source needs to be preserved somewhere if we're going to use it.
 2. As of today, 4/10/2015, the maven-central hosted version of jarjar is 4 commits (containing 3 fixes) behind the googlecode svn repo trunk tip (svn:r138 - version:1.3 on:5/3/2012 vs svn:r142 version:1.5 on:11/8/2012)

Right now we're using a hand-published (though unaltered) trunk tip / r142 jar hosted on bintray.  See here for that process: https://github.com/pantsbuild/maven-repo/commit/782e0cb4

The initial use is covered by these reviews:

 + https://rbcommons.com/s/twitter/r/2052/

issue_title: SAM-Templates >50KB not possible?
issue_body:
So we finally hit the problem, that our SAM CloudFormation templates are larger than 50KB.
As 50KB is the limit for CloudFormation if you specify a local template, we needed to look for other solutions than using `aws cloudformation deploy --template-file ...`.

We looked into:
1. Using a template URL for `aws cloudformation deploy` - not possible, because `deploy` has no such option (https://github.com/aws/aws-cli/issues/2461)
2. Using nested stacks - not possible, because transformations in child templates aren't possible (https://github.com/awslabs/serverless-application-model/issues/90)

Are there any other options? Using `create-change-set` and `execute-change-set` instead of `deploy` comes to mind, but that sounds like more or less giving up all the benefits `deploy` offers.

issue_title: Coach Reports - change when progress starts being measured in coach reports
issue_body:
## Summary

Implementations team members were confused as to why some exercises were showing up as having progress in Recent but there ended up being no data for it when they clicked into it.

**Suggestion:** 
On coach reports, log that an exercise has been engaged with only when the learner clicked "check answer". 

That way, we avoid having exercises display on the coach's end because of learners who happen to click into an exercise and leave the page without interacting with the exercise.

A potential problem area in the future is overloading the Recent tab with content that has 0 data because of learners who like to click around & explore.

issue_title: Compatibility possible with cryptography 1.2+ ?
issue_body:
As a test matrix supporting several versions of cryptography has been built, we could work on this in order to support native system libraries.

Ubuntu 16.04: cryptography 1.2.3

Alternatively / supplementary: We shouldn't allow versions that aren't supported, which is part of the error in https://github.com/learningequality/kolibri/issues/2805

One suggestion would be to create a check that also takes the version into account. Current check does this:

```
>>> try:
...     from cryptography.hazmat.backends import default_backend
...     from cryptography import exceptions as crypto_exceptions
...     crypto_backend = default_backend()
...     from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric import rsa as crypto_rsa, padding as crypto_padding
...     from cryptography.hazmat.primitives import serialization as crypto_serialization, hashes as crypto_hashes
...     CRYPTOGRAPHY_EXISTS = True
... except:
...     CRYPTOGRAPHY_EXISTS = False
```

issue_title: Reduce duplication on ContentNode subclasses
issue_body:
There's a fair bit of duplication on the subclasses of `ContentNode`; e.g.:

- the `Attributes` list in the docstring (perhaps it can just list the extra attributes specific to this kind, and then refer to the shared attributes from the docstring for `ContentNode`
- the contents of the `to_dict` method is duplicated on a number of subclasses; perhaps it can just call `to_dict` on the parent and then add in its own fields to that dict
- the arguments to `__init__` are repeated on subclasses; instead, they could be captured in `**kwargs` and then passed along to the super via `**kwargs`.

issue_title: Tokenizer not unconsuming X in in hex mode
issue_body:
Hi,

I'm implementing the WHATWG spec with C++/Qt and using your implementation as a reference is quite useful.
But I think I just found an error, 
in tokenizer.py line 172 there is:
self.stream.unget(charStack.pop())
sorry if my Python knowledge is mistaken but here
you only pop one char from the stack but in a '#Xabc' case
where the X stands for hexadecimals you sould
pop that too as the docs says: https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/syntax.html#tokenizing-character-references
"If no characters match the range, then don't consume any characters (and unconsume the U+0023 NUMBER SIGN character and, if appropriate, the X character). This is a parse error; nothing is returned."
Best.

issue_title: No second answer after one successful conversation with encryption
issue_body:
Hi,
Sorry, I'm in a hurry so a brief description.
When I have encryption enabled (EE) in XCTU: 
I can only have one successful data exchange. That means the receiver, which is a router, receives and replies sucessfully. When I try the same operation again, without restarting the receiver, the receiver don't get any frames at all afterwards. When the receiver is a coordinator everything's fine.

issue_title: Support HAR files
issue_body:
ipwb indexer currently supports WARC files as input. [HTTP Archive (HAR)](https://dvcs.w3.org/hg/webperf/raw-file/tip/specs/HAR/Overview.html) files may also serve as a trace of HTTP communication, retained as a file. Let's allow ipwb to take HARs as input as well.

The quick solution is to use https://github.com/webrecorder/har2warc then proceed with the current logic.

issue_title: Integrate proper replay with pywb
issue_body: As of the initial code, we simply display the payload, which causes the hacky (by design) replay system to leak into the live web. Utilize the already partially integrated pywb for full proper replay. This will require modifying the code in pywb to cause the part of the program that reads the WARC content to invoke code to fetch the WARC payload from IPFS before being processed by pywb.

issue_title: Replay is broken
issue_body: When we moved to combining the header and payload digest as a single locator string, the replay engine no longer seems to be able to resolve the two digests. This is a fairly major issue, @ibnesayeed

issue_title: Specifying output file to indexer reports error if path elements are not supplied
issue_body:
```
ipwb index /some/archive.warc -o myindex.cdxj
```
reports `[Errno 2] No such file or directory: ''`.

The remedy is to supply the relative path indicator to the file (`./myindex.cdxj` instead) but in other places, like `ipwb replay myindex.cdxj`, this relative path is not necessary.

We ought to

- [ ] Be consistent with whether we require the path element in indexer and replay
- [ ] Allow files to be specified for output from the indexer with an assumed path (`foo.cdxj` specified implies `./foo.cdxj`)

issue_title: Sort CDXJ output before printing/exporting
issue_body: The CDXJ format is required(?) to be sorted but IPWB does not accomplish this. @ibnesayeed recommended to use the LC_ALL flag but something within Python would be preferable.

issue_title: Python3 and Twisted
issue_body:
Twisted now supports the Python3-style `async def` functions. This is via a new API called `ensureDeferred`.

However, the `tx` side of txaio only uses `maybeDeferred` in `txaio.as_future` and `maybeDeferred` does not support `async def`. So, we should add some logic and tests to `txaio.tx.as_future` so that it (maybe) calls `ensureDeferred` in the correct circumstances.

Either that or convince Twisted that `maybeDeferred` should do this. Or maybe we *just* need to call `ensureDeferred` for all cases?

issue_title: Data not converted to bytes when starting proxy
issue_body:
When starting a proxy with python3, an exception is thrown saying that the data must not be unicode:

```
2017-09-12T14:19:58+0200 Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/twisted/python/log.py", line 103, in callWithLogger
    return callWithContext({"system": lp}, func, *args, **kw)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/twisted/python/log.py", line 86, in callWithContext
    return context.call({ILogContext: newCtx}, func, *args, **kw)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/twisted/python/context.py", line 122, in callWithContext
    return self.currentContext().callWithContext(ctx, func, *args, **kw)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/twisted/python/context.py", line 85, in callWithContext
    return func(*args,**kw)
--- <exception caught here> ---
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/twisted/internet/asyncioreactor.py", line 136, in _readOrWrite
    why = method()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/twisted/internet/tcp.py", line 586, in doConnect
    self._connectDone()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/twisted/internet/tcp.py", line 615, in _connectDone
    self.protocol.makeConnection(self)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/twisted/internet/protocol.py", line 494, in makeConnection
    self.connectionMade()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/autobahn/twisted/websocket.py", line 94, in connectionMade
    self._connectionMade()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/autobahn/websocket/protocol.py", line 3328, in _connectionMade
    self.startProxyConnect()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/autobahn/websocket/protocol.py", line 3354, in startProxyConnect
    self.sendData(request)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/autobahn/websocket/protocol.py", line 1297, in sendData
    self.transport.write(data)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/twisted/internet/_newtls.py", line 191, in write
    FileDescriptor.write(self, bytes)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/twisted/internet/abstract.py", line 348, in write
    raise TypeError("Data must not be unicode")
builtins.TypeError: Data must not be unicode
```

The origin of this error lies in module autobahn.wamp.protocol in function startProxy (line 3359):
```python
    def startProxyConnect(self):
        """
        Connect to explicit proxy.
        """
        # construct proxy connect HTTP request
        #
        request = "CONNECT %s:%d HTTP/1.1\x0d\x0a" % (self.factory.host.encode("utf-8"), self.factory.port)
        request += "Host: %s:%d\x0d\x0a" % (self.factory.host.encode("utf-8"), self.factory.port)
        request += "\x0d\x0a"

        self.log.debug("{request}", request=request)

        self.sendData(request)
```

Where the request parameter must be of type bytes. The simple solution is to prefix the strings with a 'b':
```python
    def startProxyConnect(self):
        """
        Connect to explicit proxy.
        """
        # construct proxy connect HTTP request
        #
        request = b"CONNECT %s:%d HTTP/1.1\x0d\x0a" % (self.factory.host.encode("utf-8"), self.factory.port)
        request += b"Host: %s:%d\x0d\x0a" % (self.factory.host.encode("utf-8"), self.factory.port)
        request += b"\x0d\x0a"

        self.log.debug("{request}", request=request)

        self.sendData(request)
```

This solves the issue entirely and does not break backwards compatibility (tested it locally).
I can create a pull request if you want, although it's very easy to fix.

Cheers
- Brecht

issue_title: DatasetReaders no longer have any tqdm output on read
issue_body: This is unfortunate if you're reading a lot of data, because you just sit there not knowing how long it's going to take.  Probably the fix is to change the `lazy=False` case in `DatasetReader.read()` to use `tqdm` instead of `ensure_list`.  I'll do this soon unless someone tells me there's a better way to do this.

issue_title: Instrument name parsing fails in certain cases
issue_body:
`print(instrument.fromString('Clarinetti in A.'))` returns `Alto`
`print(instrument.fromString('Clarinetti in B.'))` returns `Bass`

The dot `.` at the end mixes it up.

Something like [Levenshtein distance](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Levenshtein_distance) might be useful for finding the best match.

issue_title: Add Hungarian linking algorithm.
issue_body: Way back in #108 (June 2014) I mentioned my interest in integrating the Hungarian algorithm with trackpy. As illustrated in [Jaqaman 2008](http://www.nature.com/nmeth/journal/v5/n8/abs/nmeth.1237.html), it handles trajectories that split and merge. I recently received an email from a trackpy user who desires this feature, and it reminded me of my interest in it. I think I still have a very old branch somewhere with about 50% of the work done. Anyway, I couldn't find a dedicated issue for this -- just my old aside in #108 -- so this is one.

issue_title: Application SSL does not support SAN certificates
issue_body:
When I register a SAN certificate using `deis certs:add` only the common name is picked up, not the other SANs (which can be entierly different domain names)

An example certificate can have: `CN=example.com` and `SAN=DNS:example.co.uk,DNS:example.no,DNS:example.io,DNS:my-aweseome-example.com,DNS:other-website.com`

See this for a guide on how to create SAN certificates: http://apetec.com/support/GenerateSAN-CSR.htm

My (hopefully temporary) workaround is to just use platform SSL certificates for now and remove the application SSL solution from my `deis-router`.

issue_title: deis config and releases are inconsistent

issue_title: Rewrite Builder shell scripts in Go
issue_body:
The current builder is written in shell scripts. While some of the shell should remain, the majority of it should be reimplemented in Go for the following reasons:

- We get finer grained control over interoperability with etcd and other services
- We can write good tests!
- The platform becomes more consistent.

This is part of the overarching milestone described in #3758.

issue_title: Problem with apps that can run on configurable port
issue_body:
Take our own example-ruby-sinatra app as an example:

https://github.com/deis/example-ruby-sinatra

Its `Procfile` reads as follows:

```
web: bundle exec ruby web.rb -p ${PORT:-5000}
```

This permits the port that the Sinatra app runs on within the container to be configured via the `$PORT` environment variable.

Try the following:

```
$ git clone git@github.com:deis/example-ruby-sinatra.git
$ cd example-ruby-sinatra
$ deis create
$ deis config:set PORT=5001
$ git push deis master
$ deis open
```

Observe that you get a 502 from the router.

This happens (at least) with both the Fleet and k8s schedulers.

Digging into the reason, it seems the publisher may (I haven't confirmed) do a `docker inspect` of containers in order to ascertain what port is exposed and takes this into consideration when publishing.  Likewise (confirmed) the k8s scheduler module for the controller also does a `docker inspect` to find what port is exposed, and takes this into consideration when defining the app's service.  In either case, both believe that the application is running on port 5000 (because that is what is exposed), even though this may not actually be the case at all.

It's difficult to say what the right resolution is for this.  (I think) builder generates the Dockerfile for each app (unless an app provides their own) and _this_ is what dictates what port is exposed.  I don't know how we could make builder smart enough to detect what port an app would _actually_ run on if environment variables might be playing a role in that...

Maybe the real issue is that at least one example app hints this can be done, when in fact it doesn't work.

issue_title: Allowing multiple peer subscribers can be problematic
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

A `p2p.Peer` can have multiple subscribers, and each of them will get a copy of every msg sent by the remote (which allows us to have separate services handling different subsets of messages), but that can be problematic if both subscribers request/process the same type of data as the data requested by one subscriber will be delivered to the other as well, and might interfere other's operation.

As an example, we currently have `LightChainSyncer` and `LightPeerChain` as peer subscribers when we run a light node, and they can both request/process `BlockHeader`s, and if the latter requests some headers while the former is in the middle of a sync with a peer, it may try to validate/process those headers and if that fails (which can happen when the headers are further ahead of our local chain head), it will cause the sync with that peer to be aborted. Although this is not that big a deal given that another sync will be triggered as soon as one of our peers notify us of a new block, it is something we need to be aware of. And we must consider other cases where similar issues might happen

The above can be seen in practice when running `tests/p2p/test_lightchain_integration.py` as it requests the remote's head (via `LightPeerChain`) while `LightChainSyncer` is in the middle of a sync, which causes the sync to be aborted.

### How can it be fixed

In the case above, it could be fixed by having `LightChainSyncer` keep the `request_id`s used in the `GetBlockHeader` requests sent by itself and only processing the replies for those, but that won't work for services using the `ETH` protocols because there the requests don't include a `request_id`

I need to think more about this, just writing it down now so I don't forget about it

I worked with Docker (on my machine) and docker swarm (for cluster deployment) with last version of ethereum, web3.py, docker..

issue_title: Gas price generation using time based method generating low gas prices
issue_body:
* Version: 4.3.0
* Python: 3.6.5
* OS: win
* Node: https://mainnet.infura.io/FiZkoo##Isk3ke######

### What was wrong?
I am using sendRawTransaction to send my transaction to main network using infura node. 
Function returns valid transaction hash, but this hash can't be found on etherscan. 
Few times I got an error message that transaction is under priced, but this is not always the case. 
In most cases it just looks like transaction was send without problems. No error is being returned.

To estimate gas price I am using this code:
```python
            web3.eth.setGasPriceStrategy(medium_gas_price_strategy)
            gasprice = web3.eth.generateGasPrice()

```
It always returns 1 gwei. This is below Infura gas price limit. 

I did a test and changed my code to use 4 gwei, 10 gwei and 20 gwei. In this case transaction was successfully send to blockchain, but then after using generateGasPrice my problem returned. 

Below is the code I use to call sendRawTransaction. 

```python
from web3 import Web3, HTTPProvider
from web3.contract import ConciseContract
from web3.auto import w3
from web3.middleware import geth_poa_middleware
from web3.gas_strategies.time_based import fast_gas_price_strategy, slow_gas_price_strategy,medium_gas_price_strategy


    file = open('contractdef.js',"r")
    abiData = json.loads(file.read())
    contract_address = we3.toChecksumAddress(app.config['ETHEREUM_CONTRACT'])
    contract_instance = we3.eth.contract(contract_address,abi=abiData)

    w3.eth.enable_unaudited_features()
    we3 = Web3(HTTPProvider(app.config['PROVIDER']))
    we3.eth.enable_unaudited_features()
    we3.middleware_stack.inject(geth_poa_middleware, layer=0)

    we3.eth.setGasPriceStrategy(medium_gas_price_strategy)
    gasprice = we3.eth.generateGasPrice()
    
    owner_address = app.config['PUBLIC_ADDRESS']
    claimed_address = app.config['CLAIMED_ADDRESS']

    amount = 10
    
    gas = contract_instance.functions.transfer(claimed_address,amount).estimateGas({'from':owner_address})
    nonce = we3.eth.getTransactionCount(we3.toChecksumAddress(owner_address),'pending')

    txn = contract_instance.functions.transfer(claim.claimed_address,amount).buildTransaction({
            'from':owner_address,
            'chainId':1,
            'gas':gas,
            'gasPrice':gasprice,
            'nonce': nonce
            })

    private_key = app.config['PRIVATE_KEY']
    signed_txn = we3.eth.account.signTransaction(txn,private_key=private_key)

    tx = we3.eth.sendRawTransaction(we3.toHex(signed_txn.rawTransaction))
    print ('Transaction Number:',we3.toHex(tx))

```



This gives me result as:

`
Transaction Number: 0x5fd2e7724d7581879db346ea5b9b8cd81a424f6fe3ee08e2287964a91d66f72b
`
But, I can't find this transaction on etherscan. Even if I wait one day. 

There are two problems:

1. generateGasPrice returns wrong estimiated gasPrice ( 1 gwei seems like impossible)
2. sendRawTransaction doesn't throw errror when transaction is underpriced. It happened few times, but usually there is nothing.  


I don't know if this Infura problem or web3.py. I don't get any error message from web3.py after calling sendRawTransaction so maybe the problem is here. I will ask Infura as well.

issue_title: conda doesn't detect a failed download (v. 4.1.6)
issue_body: This was a wild goose chase. Note the download % jumps from 21% to 100% here. The error message is then: ``` Error: MD5 sums mismatch for download ..... ``` We spent a lot of time trying to re-mirror this package a few times before noticing that the download was finishing at exactly 30 seconds which is the gunicorn timeout on the webserver serving the file. Why is conda ignoring the truncated error condition? In a perfect world, it would restart the download using ranges to request the rest of the file.

issue_title: Add hash characters to tarball file name and extracted package directory name
issue_body:
Append the first 5 characters of the tarball md5 hash to the tarball file name when it's written to the package cache.  Also use the same file name (i.e. with the appended md5 characters) as the extracted package directory name.

To clarify, for the tarball file name, the hash should be appended to file name root, before the `.tar.bz2` extension.  So if the hash characters `a1b2c` for the file `cryptography-1.5.3-py27_0.tar.bz2`, the filename written to the cache should be `cryptography-1.5.3-py27_0-a1b2c.tar.bz2` and the extracted directory name should be `cryptography-1.5.3-py27_0-a1b2c`.

I think the `-` separator is correct here.  If it causes a problem with people string parsing file names for information they should be getting from metadata anyway, ¯\\\_(ツ)\_/¯.
