issue_title: Unlinking packages ... An unexpected error has occurred
Discussion for a conda plugin framework
issue_body:
I'd like to open a discussion on adding a plugin framework to conda.  What library should we use--or what patterns should we follow?  What have been others' experience with plugin frameworks?  What are best practices?

For libraries, I'm aware of [PluginBase](http://pluginbase.pocoo.org/) and [Yapsy](http://yapsy.sourceforge.net/).  I'd also consider setuptools entry_points a form of library.

Also, in what areas of conda's workflow should we consider adding shims to customize behavior?  How would the community envision using this feature, and what sorts of plugins would you want to (be able to) write?

issue_title: Change conda's self-update model
issue_body:
Conda currently aggressively auto-updates itself by default.  Alternately, the configuration

    conda config --set auto_update_conda false

disables the auto-update.  However, the auto-update functionality *only* works when you're operating on the root environment.  If you're being a good condite and making extensive use of environments, you'll rarely operate on the root environment, and thus often be running on old conda versions without knowledge that you need to update.

To give control back to users on when they update, along with letting users of environments know that their conda is out of date, ditch the aggressive auto-update conda, and instead just print a warning to the terminal when a new conda version is available.

issue_title: conda install --force implies --no-deps
issue_body:
This is a (breaking) change in behaviour  in 4.0.7. These two options should be connected the other way around (--no-deps implies --force), because there are use cases for --force with dependencies (for example an equivalent to install-or-update).

It is not very nice to have to update my scripts to check whether a particular package was installed first (and then call update) so as to restore the old behaviour, especially considering that it takes a few extra seconds.

issue_title: Allow activating multiple environments
issue_body:
Hi,
I'm trying to configure conda to use it on a computing cluster with command line access for multiple users (~300 accounts).
The idea is that we will provide to users some preinstalled conda environments that they can use as they need.

Some people will want to activate several of these environments to perform their compute task. The problem is that the `activate` script calls `deactivate` before doing the activation job, so users can only have 1 environment loaded.

I know that activating multiple environments could be a problem as some incompatibility could arise depending on the packages installed in each env. But in our case, in many situations, it would work perfectly well (and if it's not, users would be able to take care of it).

So I'm wondering if you think this feature could be added to conda somehow? Maybe with a special option for the activate script, and a modification to deactivate to allow it to deactivate _all_ the loaded env.
I can work on a PR if you are ok with the idea.

The other option would be to force them to adopt the proper conda way (they create their own environments), but it would make the transition too hard for most of them...

                        C:\Users\tsnyder\Miniconda3\envs).
  -p PATH, --prefix PATH
                        Full path to environment prefix (default:
                        C:\Users\tsnyder\Miniconda3).

  --force               force creation of environment (removing a previously
                        existing environment of the same name).
  --json                Report all output as json. Suitable for using conda
                        programmatically.
  --debug               Show debug output.
  --verbose, -v         Use once for info, twice for debug.

examples:
    conda env create
    conda env create -n name
    conda env create vader/deathstar
    conda env create -f=/path/to/environment.yml
    conda env create -f=/path/to/requirements.txt -n deathstar
    conda env create -f=/path/to/requirements.txt -p /home/user/software/deathstar
PS C:\Users\tsnyder> conda env create tsnyder/python_training_2016_10
An unexpected error has occurred.
Please consider posting the following information to the
conda GitHub issue tracker at:

    https://github.com/conda/conda/issues



Current conda install:

               platform : win-64
          conda version : 4.2.9
       conda is private : False
      conda-env version : 4.2.9
    conda-build version : not installed
         python version : 3.5.2.final.0
       requests version : 2.11.1
       root environment : C:\Users\tsnyder\Miniconda3  (writable)
    default environment : C:\Users\tsnyder\Miniconda3
       envs directories : C:\Users\tsnyder\Miniconda3\envs
          package cache : C:\Users\tsnyder\Miniconda3\pkgs
           channel URLs : https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/free/win-64/
                          https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/free/noarch/
                          https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/pro/win-64/
                          https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/pro/noarch/
                          https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/msys2/win-64/
                          https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/msys2/noarch/
            config file : None
           offline mode : False



`$ C:\Users\tsnyder\Miniconda3\Scripts\conda-env-script.py create tsnyder/python_training_2016_10`




    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "C:\Users\tsnyder\Miniconda3\lib\site-packages\conda\exceptions.py", line 473, in conda_exception_handler
        return_value = func(*args, **kwargs)
      File "C:\Users\tsnyder\Miniconda3\lib\site-packages\conda_env\cli\main_create.py", line 81, in execute
        directory=os.getcwd())
      File "C:\Users\tsnyder\Miniconda3\lib\site-packages\conda_env\specs\__init__.py", line 23, in detect
        raise SpecNotFound(build_message(specs))
    conda_env.exceptions.SpecNotFound: Runtime error: Please install binstar
    Please install nbformat:
        conda install nbformat
    Conda Env Exception: environment.yml file not found
    There is no requirements.txt
```

# What to fix?
1. This shouldn't be an "unexpected" error.  If `conda env create` takes a positional parameter named `remote_definiton` and you don't have the remote client installed, that should be an expected error that has a cleaner message.   Something that says `'{} could not be resolved but you do not have the remote client installed.  Please install {}'.format(remote_definition, remote_client)` might be appropriate.
1. It shouldn't be suggesting to install `binstar` anymore.  I vaguely remember that the latest `binstar` says "Don't use me anymore" but the UX would be nicer if we didn't send people down that road.

# How did I run into this?
I'm prepping my userbase for a course that @msarahan is going to be teaching them next week.  I'll be advising them to use our Wakari Enterprise install or JupyterHub or ipython in a central environment that I have setup for the purpose of this course.  However, I do want to let people know how easy it is to get Anaconda python working on their own systems.  So, I'm putting together the instructions on how to do a clean install of miniconda + the environment they need.  Since I want to show off Anaconda Repo, I'm trying to do `conda env create` with a `remote_definition` for the first time and noticed the exception mentions installing `binstar`.

issue_title: pysam write appears to truncate files
issue_body:
Hi there,

I would like to split a very large bam file into subfiles, one for each chromosome and each with an appropriate header. Using pysam I have come up with the following:

```
#! /usr/bin/python

import pysam

PySam_test = pysam.AlignmentFile('test.bam')

chromosomes = zip(PySam_test.references, PySam_test.lengths)

def split_PySam_test_by_chromosome(chromosome):
    outfile = pysam.AlignmentFile("test_%s.bam" % chromosome[0], "wb", reference_names=[chromosome[0]], reference_lengths=[chromosome[1]])
    for alignment in PySam_test.fetch(reference=chromosome[0]): outfile.write(alignment)
    outfile.close()

map(split_PySam_test_by_chromosome, chromosomes)
```

Unfortunately, this seems to work for the first subfile only (i.e. test_chr1.bam). All the remaining files are written to disk and appear to have roughly the appropriate size, however, samtools view -h prints the header and then gives a truncated file error, e.g.: 

```
@SQ     SN:2    LN:242193529 
[main_samview] truncated file.
```

Looks as though the end of file is not indicated properly, but why does it work for the first file but not all the others when all of them are generated from the same loop?

Any help would be much appreciated.

Cheers,

mce

PS: In the end, I would like to make that run in paralell, i.e.

```
import multiprocessing
cpus = multiprocessing.cpu_count()

pool = multiprocessing.Pool(cpus)
pool.map(split_PySam_by_chromosome, chromosomes)
pool.close()
```
Hence the map statement.

issue_title: Curator fails to delete index when multiple ES hosts are configured
issue_body:
My `curator.yaml` config file contains the following:

```yaml
---
client:
  hosts:
    - http://elasticsearch-1
    - http://elasticsearch-2
    - http://elasticsearch-3
  port: 80
  use_ssl: False
  ssl_no_validate: False
  timeout: 30
  master_only: False

logging:
  loglevel: DEBUG
  logformat: default
  blacklist: ['elasticsearch', 'urllib3']
```

When I run using this configuration, it fails with the following:

```
2018-03-29 10:07:43,359 INFO      curator.actions.delete_indices           __chunk_loop:502  ---deleting index elasticsearch-3-2018.03.19

2018-03-29 10:07:43,389 ERROR                curator.cli                    run:184  Failed to complete action: delete_indices.  <class 'curator.exceptions.FailedExecution'>: Exception encountered.  Rerun with loglevel DEBUG and/or check Elasticsearch logs for more information. Exception: TransportError(404, 'index_not_found_exception', 'no such index')
```

I have checked `elasticsearch-3` host and the index `elasticsearch-3-2018.03.19` is indeed still present there (curator couldn't delete it, neither the other ones that it was supposed to delete).

However, if I change my `curator.yaml` and remove `elasticsearch-1` and `elasticsearch-2` hosts, it runs successfully.

```
2018-03-29 10:11:19,059 INFO      curator.actions.delete_indices           __chunk_loop:502  ---deleting index elasticsearch-3-2018.03.19
2018-03-29 10:11:19,578 DEBUG              curator.utils            get_indices:644  Detected Elasticsearch version 5.6.5
2018-03-29 10:11:19,578 DEBUG     curator.actions.delete_indices         _verify_result:489  Successfully deleted all indices on try #1
```
It might be the case where its trying to look for `elasticsearch-3-2018.03.19` in the incorrect ES host but I'm not sure (i.e. trying to find a index from `elasticsearch-3` inside of `elasticsearch-2` host).

### Expected Behavior
Curator deletes all indexes based on my `actions.yaml` pattern on all hosts inside the `hosts:` key of my `curator.yaml` configuration file.

### Actual Behavior
Curator starts with the latest host of my configuration file and fails when trying to delete the oldest index (based on my retention period configuration).

### Steps to Reproduce the Problem
  1. Create a curator config file with 2 or more Elasticsearch hosts
  2. Run curator trying to delete some indexes

### Specifications
Linux, tested on different distros, including inside Docker.
Elasticsearch version 5.6.5
Curator 5.5.1

Thanks!

issue_title: Subcommand for returning a valid accessToken
issue_body:
`gcloud config config-helper` returns a JSON blob with a number of things about the current configuration of the CLI, along with an properly-refreshed, active token for applications to use. This is a feature request for a similar functionality in the `az` CLI.

Scenario: In Kubernetes, we are trying to emulate the Google Auth plugin which uses `gcloud config config-helper` to get a refreshed accessToken and then uses it for authentication in it's own ways. Currently, the Azure implementation reaches into `~/.azure/` directly to read the access token. It would be much better if this was done via os.Exec'ing `az config config-helper` or something similar, so that the CLI can refresh the token if needed and then return it.

Can we create a command like `config config-helper` in `az`?

cc: @brendandburns

issue_title: az keyvault recover does not work
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
The `az keyvault recover` command does not work. Even though a keyvault is listed in `az keyvault list-deleted`, the `recover` command fails with `The Resource 'Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults/${keyvaultname}' not found within subscription.`

This appears to be a misapplication of the `validate_resource_group_name` function in `command_modules/keyvault/_validators.py`.

**To Reproduce**
- Create a keyvault, then turn on soft deletes.
- Delete the keyvault.
- Try to recover the keyvault using `az keyvault recover`,

**Expected behavior**
`az keyvault recover` should validate against deleted keyvault names, not current keyvault names.

**Environment summary**
Azure CLI on Ubuntu 16.04:
```
ii  azure-cli                                  2.0.44-1~xenial                              all          Azure CLI
```

**Additional context**
This is almost certainly not an environment-specific problem and should be easy to reproduce.

issue_title: Tutorial for Multicloud not working
issue_body:
Hi there,

The tutorial for multicloud connections (https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/blob/master/doc/multicloud.rst) does not work.

This is due to the fact that overwriting the auth_uri does not result in using the complete string as the new auth_endpoint uri. It always results in the URL builder function _build_url() creating a auth URL from the variable self.auth_endpoint, which is always the default azure global one.

I have already created an issue in the msrestazure project to implement a "fix" for that, but I am asking myself why this is part of the documentation, even though it is not working as described.

Regards

issue_title: Incorrect dependency on azure-storage-common
issue_body:
Current dependency on azure-storage-common is defined as:
```python
'azure-storage-common>=1.1.0,<1.2.0'
```
This is not the semver recommendation. According to semver, minor version cannot be broken version, and then pin at the bug fix version is incorrect.
This makes your package impossible to install with azure-storage 1.3.0 released yesterday, even if versioning says it's possible.

issue_title: MSIAuthentication should set toekn on instance creation
issue_body:
All msrestazure instance set the token on instance creation, except MSIAuthentication.

This presents a few issues:
- Unlike all other usages, authentication exception will be raised at usage, not at creation level
- This creates weird bug in KeyVault which is doing some magic inside credentials:
https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/issues/2852

issue_title: xargs mode?
issue_body:
I sometimes come across projects (for example [import-sort](https://github.com/renke/import-sort)) that only take 1 file at a time, and error when pre-commit tries to throw n files at it

in such cases, i've resorted to a wrapper script to call it sequentially

could there a better way to do this?

I'd like to write something like
```
-   id: reorder-es6-imports
    name: Reorder ES6 Imports
    description: This hook reorders ES6 imports.
    entry: import-sort
    language: node
    types: [javascript]
    args: [--overwrite]

    # Use additional_dependencies to install the actual node package
    additional_dependencies: ['import-sort-cli@^3.3.0', 'import-sort-parser-babylon@^3.2.0', 'import-sort-style-eslint@^3.0.0']
    minimum_pre_commit_version: 0.7.0

    # this flag flag does not exist, but it would be neat if it did :)
    pass_files_sequentially: true
```

(I'm thinking the same way there's a `pass_filenames` flag, we could have a `pass_files_sequentially` flag or something)

Fwiw we can achieve the same with xargs:

```
-   id: reorder-es6-imports
    name: Reorder ES6 Imports
    description: This hook reorders ES6 imports.
    entry: bash -c 'echo "$@" | xargs -n1 import-sort --overwrite'
    language: node
    types: [javascript]

    # Use additional_dependencies to install the actual node package
    additional_dependencies: ['import-sort-cli@^3.3.0', 'import-sort-parser-babylon@^3.2.0', 'import-sort-style-eslint@^3.0.0']
    minimum_pre_commit_version: 0.7.0
```

However:
- xargs isn't very portable
- `args` does seem to be respected
- we have to do this wrapping

I think a new flag to solve these things would be nice - @asottile what do you think?

issue_body:
## Long story short
I was firing around 6000 requests concurrently and after some amount of time, the program is terminated without any error.

## Expected behaviour

The program should run and all the files should be downloaded

## Actual behaviour

The program is terminated without error

## Steps to reproduce
I have only one channel and the channel has around 6000 files

```
import asyncio
import os
import ssl
import sys
import time
from configparser import ConfigParser
import aiohttp
from aiohttp import ClientSession


async def fetch_files(channel):
    global files
    tasks = []
    files = []
    async with ClientSession(connector=aiohttp.TCPConnector(ssl_context=get_ssl_context())) as session:
        try:
            url = config.get(env, 'cron.url.channel.files').replace("${channel}", channel)
            task = asyncio.ensure_future(fetch(url, session, "json"))
            tasks.append(task)
        except Exception as e:
            print("The error occurred while processing ", str(e))
        files = await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)
        if len(files) > 0:
            channel_dir = folder_name + "/" + channel
            if not os.path.exists(channel_dir):
                os.makedirs(channel_dir)
            futures = [fetch_file_data(channel, file, session) for file in files[0]]
            await asyncio.ensure_future(asyncio.gather(*futures, return_exceptions=True))


async def fetch(url, session, req_type):
    async with session.get(url) as response:
        print("Response status code for the url ", url, " is ", response.status)
        if response.status == 200:
            if req_type is "json":
                return await response.json()
            else:
                return await response.text()
        else:

        print(url)
        response = await fetch(url, session, "text")
        if response is not None:
            with open(file_path, "w+") as f:
                f.write(response)
    except asyncio.TimeoutError as r:
        print("Timeout, skipping ", url , " and the exception is ", r.message)
    except Exception as e:
        print("Error occured while processing ", str(e))


def get_env():

    return "test"


def get_ssl_context():
    global ssl_context
    ssl_context = ssl.SSLContext(ssl.PROTOCOL_SSLv23)
    ssl_context.load_cert_chain(config.get(env, 'cron.crt.file'), config.get(env, 'cron.key.file'))
    return ssl_context


files = []
env = ""
ssl_context = ""
config = ""
folder_name = ""


def main(args):
    start = time.time()
    global config
    global env
    global files
    global folder_name
    channel_list = []
    files = []
    env = get_env()
    if len(args) >= 3:
        folder_name = args[1]
        if not os.path.exists(folder_name):
            os.makedirs(folder_name)
        for x in args[2:]:
            channel_list.append(x)
        config = ConfigParser()
        config.read('config.ini')
        try:
            loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
            loop.set_debug(True)
            futures = [fetch_files(channel) for channel in channel_list]
            result = asyncio.ensure_future(asyncio.gather(*futures, return_exceptions=True))
            loop.run_until_complete(result)
        except asyncio.CancelledError:
            print("aiohttp cancelled")
        except:
            print("Closed abruptly")
        finally:

    else:

    times = time.time() - start
    print("time ", times, "seconds ie ", times/60, " minutes")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main(sys.argv)

Python 3.5
aiohttp 2.3.9

issue_title: access_log_class can't be used in handle_args of aiohttp.web.Application
issue_body:
## Long story short

I'm setting up a project with a custom access logger. My code looks like:

```python
# main.py
import asyncio

from aiohttp import web
from aiohttp.abc import AbstractAccessLogger


class AccessLogger(AbstractAccessLogger):

    def log(self, request, response, time):
        self.logger.info(f'{request.remote} '
                         f'"{request.method} {request.path} '
                         f'done in {time}s: {response.status}')


loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()

app = web.Application(
    loop=loop,
    handler_args={
         'access_log_class': AccessLogger,
    },
)
```

When I run it with gunicorn:
```
gunicorn main:app --bind localhost:8080 --worker-class aiohttp.worker.GunicornWebWorker
```

An error is raised:
```
TypeError: type object got multiple values for keyword argument 'access_log_class'
```

Turns out `handler_args` overrides every keyword argument of `Application.make_handler()`, except `access_log_class`.

## Expected behaviour

Using `access_log_class` in `handler_args` and my given value replace the default `aiohttp.helpers.AccessLogger`.

## Actual behaviour

```
TypeError: type object got multiple values for keyword argument 'access_log_class'
```

## Steps to reproduce

Setup a file with the contents of `main.py` above and run it with the `gunicorn` command above.

## Your environment

python 3.6.6
```
$ pip freeze
aiohttp==3.3.2
async-timeout==3.0.0
attrs==18.1.0
chardet==3.0.4
gunicorn==19.9.0
idna==2.7
idna-ssl==1.1.0
multidict==4.3.1
yarl==1.2.6
```

issue_body: I'm using Jupyter notebook with anaconda and yesterday updated the iPython kernel. Before, when I plotted something by default I got a fairly small figure like below: Suddenly, the figures now are really big (by default take up the whole width of the browser) and I can't really scale them down, at least not in a pretty way. I'm using `%matplotlib inline` and for example when I specify `plt.rcParams['figure.figsize'] = (10,6)` I get a nice large figure as below: However, if I'm trying to make the figure smaller, e.g. with `plt.rcParams['figure.figsize'] = (2,1.2)`, while I do get a smaller figure, it looks awful as it doesn't scale accordingly: the code that's used to generate the figures is the following: ``` def plot_mnist(X, y, X_test=None, y_test=None, title=None): plt.figure() colorlist = get_colors(10) # Scale and visualize the embedding vectors x_min, x_max = np.min(X, 0), np.max(X, 0) if (X_test is not None) and (y_test is not None): x_min, x_max = np.min(np.array([x_min, np.min(X_test, 0)]), 0), np.max(np.array([x_max, np.max(X_test, 0)]), 0) X_test = (X_test - x_min) / (x_max - x_min) X = (X - x_min) / (x_max - x_min) if (X_test is not None) and (y_test is not None): for i in range(X_test.shape[0]): plt.text(X_test[i, 0], X_test[i, 1], str(y_test[i]), color=colorlist[y_test[i]], fontdict={'weight': 'medium', 'size': 'smaller'}, alpha=0.4) for i in range(X.shape[0]): plt.text(X[i, 0], X[i, 1], str(y[i]), color=colorlist[y[i]], fontdict={'weight': 'medium', 'size': 'smaller'}, alpha=1.) plt.xticks([]), plt.yticks([]) plt.xlim(-0.05, 1.05) plt.ylim(-0.05, 1.05) if title is not None: plt.title(title) ``` and since there are no absolute specifications for font size etc I would expect it to scale down accordingly.... Am I doing something wrong here or is there a bug somewhere? Since the figures scale nicely when I decrease the width of the browser window, I'm assuming the nice way to specify the size of the figures would be to tell the notebook how much of the default width of the browser window it should use for the figure but I'm not sure if or where I could find such a setting?

issue_title: Handle cases when there are marked cells off-screen
issue_body:
The user could potentially do things they don't intend to (e.g. delete multiple cells) if there are marked cells off screen. This might also be something they do intend to do, though, so I would say we shouldn't prevent users from taking actions on off-screen marked cells, but that we should at least give them a heads up that this is the case. I have some ideas for this:

* add something to the notification area
* have a little banner at the top or bottom of the screen (kind of like what gitter has for unread messages)
* put a counter or something somewhere near the current cell selector that displays the total number of marked cells (but only display it if the number is greater than 1)

Thoughts?

issue_title: Differentiation with np.select (piecewise functions)
issue_body:
You're doing some great work here! I've run into a slight issue with computing gradients of piecewise functions using `np.select`. I've put together a minimal (trivial) example (Python 3.3):

```python
import autograd.numpy as anp
from autograd import grad

def func(x):
    return anp.select([True], [x[0]*(anp.log(x[1]**x[1]) + anp.log(x[2]**x[2]))])

gradient = grad(func)(anp.array([300., 0.5, 0.5]))
print(gradient)
```
```
[ 0.  0.  0.]
[...]/autograd/core.py:39: UserWarning: Output seems independent of input. Returning zero gradient.
  warnings.warn("Output seems independent of input. Returning zero gradient.")
```

`select` accepts a list of conditions and a list of choices, and the first condition to be evaluated `True` is returned from the corresponding choice list. The gradient of a piecewise-defined function should just be the gradient of the choice list, but I haven't yet wrapped my head around how constructing a closure for `defgrad` would work in this context.

issue_title: Stochastic gradient estimation
issue_body:
Hey,
Are there any future plans to implement some sort of stochastic gradient estimation into autograd? I am talking about something like [stochastic grad estimation on comp graphs](http://arxiv.org/abs/1506.05254) and [MuProp](http://arxiv.org/abs/1511.05176). So we could propagate gradients through pretty much anything.

Would this be a good use to time? Does it make sense? Is it plausible?

Anyway, I was thinking of getting into this over summer (southern hemisphere), so let me know.
Alex

issue_title: Fast bootstrapping for server
issue_body:
We now have fast bootstrapping functionality in the server (great work, @jcnelson!) and should probably expose this to the users. I think having a "--fast" flag when starting the server can be one way to do this.

Here are some next steps:
1. Start supporting the "--fast" flag as a command-line option when starting the server. 
2. Setup a default repository from where the server will fetch a copy of untrusted DB (can be the same server as apt-get package repository).
3. Allow the user to change the default untrusted DB location in settings.
4. Ask the user for a trusted consensus hash and run verifydb.

I'm thinking that step (4) above can be optional. As in, if I'm doing an internal deployment and trust the DB that will be downloaded on a new VM then I don't need to run verifydb.

Side note: Ethereum has a somewhat similar ["--fast" option](http://ethereum.stackexchange.com/questions/1984/what-is-the-difference-between-geth-and-geth-fast) as well.

issue_title: info should display an attempted transaction's last error
issue_body: When the blockstack registrar is attempting to process transactions, `blockstack info` returns the current state machine. However, if a persistent error (e.g., wallet does not have enough coin to subsidize the transaction) is preventing the registrar from moving to the next state, it should provide an error message.

issue_title: STACKs: Namespaces will be paid for with STACKs
issue_body:
When STACKs go live, namespaces will be purchased in STACKs.

* When sending a `NAMESPACE_PREORDER` transaction, the payer sends a dust amount to the default burn address instead of the price in Bitcoin.
* A `NAMESPACE_REVEAL` transaction is only accepted if the `NAMESPACE_PREORDER` payer address has enough STACKs.
* When a `NAMESPACE_REVEAL` transaction is committed, the `NAMESPACE_PREORDER` payer is debited the cost of the namespace in STACKs.

The namespace price curve is structured the same as it is in Bitcoin, with four price buckets.  The opening exchange rate for STACKs is 1 STACK = $0.15 USD.  The prices for namespaces in STACKs is structured to be as close as it was in September 2015, when Blockstack migrated to Bitcoin.

| Namespace length | micro-STACKs | Approx. cost in USD |
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
| 1 character | 640000000000 | $96,000 |
| 2-3 characters | 64000000000 | $9,600 |
| 4-7 characters | 6400000000 | $960 |
| 8+ characters | 640000000 | $96 |

Note that there are 1e6 micro-STACKs in 1 STACK (i.e. STACKs can subdivided into 1e6 units).

issue_title: Skip test_pylast.py if env vars not found
issue_body:
I've re-enabled PR builds for https://travis-ci.org/pylast/pylast/

The integration tests test_pylast.py won't run on PRs because there are secure env variables.

test_pylast.py should be modified to just skip if the env vars can't be found rather than erroring/failing. That way at least the unit tests, which don't require env vars, can be run.

(Perhaps pylast should have a develop branch -- PRs can be merged into there, the integration tests run, and if passed, merged to master.)

issue_title: Ignore "_" vars in "Unused Variable" check
issue_body:
*Original report by [archardlias](https://launchpad.net/~archardlias) on [Launchpad](https://bugs.launchpad.net/bugs/1328169):*

------------------------------------

Would it be possible to ignore variables named "_" from "Unused Variable" checks?

I'm not really sure whether "_" is officially specified anywhere. But I do see it a lot and in a lot of languages including python to denote a variable to be ignored (place holder dummy). PyLint implements this pseudo(?)-convention (? second-hand knowledge).

Would be great, since I would love to be explicit about "things returned but not used" instead of just calling without assignment which looks tantamount to "this function does never return anything / does not contain a return statement". A great plus in signature visibility throughout the code, specially for first-time readers.

Best regards,
~arlias

issue_title: Comment line indentation causes ParsingError
issue_body:
Running redbaron 0.6.3. 
Parsing the snippet below
```python
try:  # get 'all' referents of objects
      # note that gc.get_referents()
      # returns () for dict...-iterators
    from gc import get_referents as _getreferents
except ImportError:  # no get_referents() in Python 2.2
    def _getreferents(unused):
        return ()  # sorry, no refs
```
throws a ParsingError.
The cause is the comments after the try statement. If you dedent the two line comments back two spaces everything parses as you would expect.

The same error appears to happen when you have an if statement where the first line is over indented with a comment

issue_title: URLField.gen_value doesn't return especially unique values
issue_body:
Method ``URLField.gen_value`](http://nailgun.readthedocs.org/en/latest/api/nailgun.entity_fields.html#nailgun.entity_fields.URLField) frequently generates identical values. As a result of this issue, several work-arounds have been added to module `nailgun.entities`. One example is at [`Media.create_missing`](http://nailgun.readthedocs.org/en/latest/api/nailgun.entities.html#nailgun.entities.Media.create_missing):

```python
    def create_missing(self):
        """Give the ``path_`` instance attribute a value if it is unset.

        By default, :meth:`nailgun.entity_fields.URLField.gen_value` does not
        return especially unique values. This is problematic, as all media must
        have a unique path.

        """
        if not hasattr(self, 'path_'):
            self.path_ = gen_url(subdomain=gen_alpha())
        return super(Media, self).create_missing()
```

Let's fix `gen_url` and drop the work-arounds from module `nailgun.entities`.

issue_title: Test Runs created by Betelgeuse should set the `isautomated` flag to True
issue_body: Since `Test Runs` created by Betelgeuse are populated with data from automated test runs, it makes sense to set the `isautomated` attribute to `True`. Perhaps this should be an option (default to `False`?) passed to the `test-run` method?

issue_title: Make sure that Test Cases set some specific fields
issue_body:
Whether creating for the first time or updating existing `Test Cases`, the following fields should be set:

- [x] Title - Short description of the test case
- [x] Status - (defaults to `DRAFT` but other acceptable values are `PROPOSED`, `NEEDS UPDATE`, `APPROVED` and `INACTIVE`)
- [x] Test Type - (defaults to `FUNCTIONAL` but other acceptable values are `NON-FUNCTIONAL` and `STRUCTURAL`)
- [x] Subtype 1 - More specific selection for certain types. (defaults to `-`)
- [x] Pos/Neg - Set this to indicate if the test is a positive or negative style test . (defaults to `POSITIVE` but other acceptable values are `NEGATIVE`)
- [x] Importance- This field should have been imported from TCMS. (defaults to `MEDIUM` but other acceptable values are `LOW, `HIGH` and `CRITICAL`)
- [x] Level - Set the level of the test based on the definitions here.Test Types, Levels, and Importance (defaults to `COMPONENT` but other acceptable values are `INTEGRATION`, `SYSTEM`and `ACCEPTANCE`)
- [x] Automation- Acceptable values are `AUTOMATED`, `MANUAL ONLY`, `NOT AUTOMATED`

As you can see, `Betelgeuse` already sets these fields but some of the defaults values used need to be flexible to allow for more flexibility.

For the time being, specially for the **Satellite 6 QE** team, the following rules should be used:

* Status: `DRAFT` as we want to be able to automatically update them whenever the docstring changes. If we were to set the `Status` to any other value, updating a `Test Case` would require several steps to change the `Status` back to `DRAFT`.
* Pos/Neg: Since all of our tests use a nomenclature similar to `test_positive/negative_*`, we should use this information when importing/updating `Test Cases`
* Automation: We currently rely on `Testimony` flag found in the docstring to determine whether something is automated or not.

No changes required for the other fields for now.

issue_title: Instruction 21h seems to have a wrong implementation
issue_body:
Hi,
I saw this flaw with the const/high16 opcode (and fixed it at least for DAD), but as i tried to fix it in DAD and also in the decompiler i found out that the instruction it self is not implemented correctly.
The instruction basically moves a 16bit value into the high part of a register, there are two versions: const/high16 and const-wide/high16, where the first one uses 32bit registers and the second 64bit.
In the current implementation only the 16bit value is returned in the opcode.BBBB variable.
A quick fix would be to just fix the value by shifting it 16 or 48 bytes in opcode.BBBB but then the name of BBBB should also be BBBB0000 and BBBB000000000000.

The fix is easy but i have no idea what else would break if you shift the value inside BBBB by the right value or if everything gets even worse if you rename it? discuss now ;)

issue_title: No Attribute NullHandler when running pytest -n
issue_body:
I just figured out a very obscure issue that manifests itself when you try to use pytest-xdist. I doubt that it can really be considered a bug, but I thought I would record it here for posterity, in case another poor soul runs into this problem.

### TLDR

There is a logging module in PyPi: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/logging, but actually logging is part of the stdlib.

If you have this module installed, pytest-xdist somehow chokes while running tests, in a surprising way that gives you the impression that xdist is broken.

### Sample Project

``` bash
➜ tree -I venv
.
└── tests
    │
    └── test_stuff.py

➜ pip freeze
apipkg==1.4
backoff==1.3.1
execnet==1.4.1
logging==0.4.9.6
py==1.4.31
pytest==3.0.3
pytest-xdist==1.15.0
wheel==0.24.0
```

tests/test_stuff.py:
```python
import unittest
import backoff

class TestFoo(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_foo(self):
        print 'ok'
        assert True
```

```
➜ py.test tests 
============================================================================== test session starts ===============================================================================
platform darwin -- Python 2.7.5, pytest-3.0.3, py-1.4.31, pluggy-0.4.0
rootdir: /Users/jonathanlafleche/workspace/fuck/pytest-bug, inifile: 
plugins: xdist-1.15.0
collected 1 items 

tests/test_stuff.py .

============================================================================ 1 passed in 0.02 seconds ============================================================================
```

```
➜ py.test -n 5 tests
============================================================================== test session starts ===============================================================================
platform darwin -- Python 2.7.5, pytest-3.0.3, py-1.4.31, pluggy-0.4.0
rootdir: /Users/jonathanlafleche/workspace/fuck/pytest-bug, inifile: 
plugins: xdist-1.15.0
gw0 [0] / gw1 [0] / gw2 [0] / gw3 [0] / gw4 [0]
scheduling tests via LoadScheduling

===================================================================================== ERRORS =====================================================================================
______________________________________________________________________ ERROR collecting tests/test_stuff.py ______________________________________________________________________
tests/test_stuff.py:2: in <module>
    import backoff
venv/lib/python2.7/site-packages/backoff.py:35: in <module>
    logger.addHandler(logging.NullHandler())  # pragma: no cover
E   AttributeError: 'module' object has no attribute 'NullHandler'
============================================================================ 1 error in 0.70 seconds =============================================================================
(venv)jonathanlafleche@MAC-10001148 in ~/workspace/fuck/pytest-bug  
```

After bashing my head against the keyboard for _a while_, I figured it out:
```
pip uninstall logging
➜ py.test -n 5 tests   
============================================================================== test session starts ===============================================================================
platform darwin -- Python 2.7.5, pytest-3.0.3, py-1.4.31, pluggy-0.4.0
rootdir: /Users/jonathanlafleche/workspace/fuck/pytest-bug, inifile: 
plugins: xdist-1.15.0
gw0 [1] / gw1 [1] / gw2 [1] / gw3 [1] / gw4 [1]
scheduling tests via LoadScheduling
.
============================================================================ 1 passed in 0.78 seconds ============================================================================
```

issue_title: `update` needs to be a selective operation.
issue_body:
Along with resolution of #608 resolution of this issue is intended to resolve #564. 

The new method `replace` will replace the old update method.    The new `update` will be selective.

Instead of `put`ing the whole configuration object in a request to the server, `update` will only submit the explicitly specified parameters in its kwargs, in a `patch` request to the server.   This feature should be implemented _after_ #608.

Once this feature/issue, _AND_ #608 have successfully landed we should revisit #564.

issue_title: Make it more clear that users need to download the VS tooling installer for .NET Core in VS.
issue_body:
I have a dotnet core 1.1 project that I recently wanted to test the first run experience with the dotnet core tooling. (i.e. reformatting a machine for this purpose).

One thing that I found very confusing for the 1.1 installer is the following:

On the main page https://www.microsoft.com/net/core you believe you are downloading only dotnet core 1.0. Since I had upgraded to 1.1, I went directly to the other download page:

https://www.microsoft.com/net/download/core

This now gives me two options: `LTS` and `Current` (**Off-topic:** Why is LTS the default here and not current?)

Now since I've installed the bits quite a few times on different machines, I thought I could just install the Windows x64 SDK installer. However after installing only that and trying to load a project, I will get an incompatible framework error on loading my project.

`The imported project "C:\Program Files(x86)\MSBuild\Microsoft\VisualStudio\v14.0\DotNet\Microsoft.DotNet.Props" was not found.Confirm that the path in the <Import> declaration is correct, and that the file exist on disk.
`

This error doesn't really help me resolve the problem. It actually makes me go down the wrong path by searching on Stack Overflow/Forums/etc. However I then went back to the original page:

https://www.microsoft.com/net/core (Which has VS 2015 selected by default)

And it points to the correct tooling that I needed to install before installing the 1.1 runtime.

https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=827546

At this point I actually didn't know there was VS Tooling. I thought it was installed with the Windows SDK, but apparently you get 1.0 **AND** VS Tooling in this installer.

Because of this and myself skipping over the "Windows" guide to just get the 1.1 SDK, it wasn't obvious that I needed the VS tooling. However after installing the dotnet core VS tooling and then installing the 1.1 SDK, everything worked fine.

**Proposal:**

1) Add a note on the downloads page(https://www.microsoft.com/net/download/core) in which a pre-requisite to using dotnet core in Visual Studio is the dotnet core visual studio tooling.

2) Empower the error message above to let the user know that they might not have the dotnet core VS tooling installed even though they have dotnet core Windows SDK installed.

issue_title: apache mode fails on ipv6 listen directive
issue_body:
Commandline used:
```bash
./letsencrypt-auto --apache -d somedomain.com
```

The client (master branch at git commit 91b9d5f30e783259bf12742e53910918a959ac7b) has issues parsing the following ports.conf that contains an ipv6 address:
```apache
Listen 80

<IfModule ssl_module>
  Listen 192.168.1.1:443
  Listen [2a01:aaa:bbb:ccc::1]:443
</IfModule>

<IfModule mod_gnutls.c>
  Listen 192.168.1.1:443
  Listen [2a01:aaa:bbb:ccc::1]:443
</IfModule>
```
The client fails with this error message: 
```
MisconfigurationError: Error while running apache2ctl configtest.
Action 'configtest' failed.
The Apache error log may have more information.

AH00526: Syntax error on line 2 of /etc/apache2/le_tls_sni_01_cert_challenge.conf:
The address or port is invalid
```
I grabbed the generated /etc/apache2/le_tls_sni_01_cert_challenge.conf file and found the following (invalid) contents: 
```apache
<VirtualHost [2a01:443>
    ServerName **hexstring**.**hexstring**.acme.invalid
    UseCanonicalName on
    SSLStrictSNIVHostCheck on

    LimitRequestBody 1048576

    Include /etc/letsencrypt/options-ssl-apache.conf
    SSLCertificateFile /var/lib/letsencrypt/*alphastring*-*alphastring*.crt
    SSLCertificateKeyFile /var/lib/letsencrypt/alphastring-alphastring.pem

    DocumentRoot /var/lib/letsencrypt/tls_sni_01_page/
</VirtualHost>
```

The IPv6 Address / Port combination [2a01:....]:443 is truncated - looks like a splt at ":" and then the first and last part are used which is correct for IPv4 but wrong for IPV6 addresses.

issue_title: Common subcommand flags help
issue_body:
Currently, when you run commands like `letsencrypt --help install`, you get the following output:
```
$ letsencrypt --help install  
usage: 
  letsencrypt [SUBCOMMAND] [options] [domains]

The Let's Encrypt agent can obtain and install HTTPS/TLS/SSL certificates.  By
default, it will attempt to use a webserver both for obtaining and installing
the cert.  

positional arguments:
  SUBCOMMAND
    run                 Obtain a certificate and install.
    auth                Authenticate & obtain cert, but do not install it.
    install             Install a previously obtained cert in a server.
    revoke              Revoke a previously obtained certificate.
    rollback            Rollback server configuration changes made during
                        install.
    config_changes      Show changes made to server config during installation
    plugins             List server software plugins.

optional arguments:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  -c CONFIG_FILE, --config CONFIG_FILE
                        config file path (default: None)

install:
  Options for modifying how a cert is deployed
```
This to me doesn't seem very helpful. The problem is arguments shared between subcommands. Most of these common flags are now in the `paths` argument subgroup. I think ideally, the common arguments should not be duplicated when running `letsencrypt --help all`, but should be shown when running help for a specific subcommand. I do not believe this behavior is achievable with `argparse` and adds a fair amount of complexity, so we shouldn't worry about doing anything like this for now.

Instead, a simple thing we could do is update the description for the relevant subgroups to point users towards the `paths` group. I'm not sure what other simple fixes could be done here, but I don't think the help for commands like `install` and `revoke` does much to help the user right now.

issue_title: Will be building a RPM package for Fedora, interested in coop?
issue_body:
Hi,

it will be necessary in our company to run our own pypiserver. To improve the work for the admins we want to build rpm packages for them. Would you guys be interested in cooperating?

Currently I have a shell script which creates a virtualenv location and a package location, starts the virtualenv and installs a new pypiserver, then runs it on a configured port. Additionally I've created a systemd service file which enables automatic startup for the package service. The next step will be putting all of that into a RPM spec file and finding a service that always builds a new package when the shell script or the service file change. I think that a public rpm builder would be okay and that the pypiserver repo would be a reasonable location for these files. What do you guys think?

issue_title: Issues with Capitalization
issue_body:
Hi!

I'm trying to serialize a schema to have the fields upper case (dealing with Amazon API stuff)

The output should be so
```
{
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "s3:ListAllMyBuckets",
        "s3:GetBucketLocation"
      ],
      "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::*"
}
```

The schema 
```
class StatementSchema(Schema):
    sid = fields.Str(attribute='Sid', data_key='Sid', default=str(uuid4()))
    effect = fields.Str(attribute='Effect', data_key='Effect', valdate=validate.OneOf(('Allow', 'Deny')))
    action = fields.Str(attribute='Action', data_key='Action', required=False, allow_none=True, many=True)
    not_action = fields.Str(attribute='NotAction', data_key='NotAction', required=False, allow_none=True, many=True)
    resource = fields.Str(attribute='Resource', data_key='Resource', required=False, allow_none=True, many=True)
    not_resource = fields.Str(attribute='NotResource', data_key='NotResource', required=False, allow_none=True,
                              many=True)
    condition = fields.Nested(fields.Dict(keys=fields.Str(), values=fields.Str()), attribute='Condition',
                              data_key='Condition')
```

I tried using a pre_dump hook to capitalize everythign
```
@pre_dump
def capitalize(self, data):

issue_title: Setting buffer size in windows
issue_body:
The function set_buffer_size in serialwin32.py, is used to set a buffer size for driver. The docstring says this function should be called before opening the port. 
But the call in line 72 of serialwin32.py uses hard coded numbers (4096) for buffer sizes which will over write these values.
either the docstring is incorrect and that function should be called after opening the port( I tested this on my machine(FDTI) and it worked)
 Or the set_buffer_size should not make a call to win32.SetupComm and just store the buffer values so they will be later used within the open routing (line 72).

btw, I'm very new to open source and would love to fix it if it's ok.

issue_title: Conventions for keeping track of creation- and modification-dates
issue_body:
An unfortunate aspect of the HDF5 format is that opening a file makes the operating system treat it as modified, even if nothing changed and even if it is opened in read-only mode. On Windows this results in a changed modification date, and on Linux and OSX there is no distinction between modification or creation dates to begin with.

It would be useful to be able to keep track of *real* changes to loom files. This could be used to trigger automatic updates in various work-flows, for example. Having different levels of granularity about what was changed would also be useful here. It would also be useful if there was a convention for this, so that modification-detecting scripts from different groups would work with each other's files without much trouble. If the `loompy` library would inherently keep track of and update these modification tracking attributes, things would be even easier, since people would not have to think about it.

To give a concrete example: to serve data from a loom file to a website, a `loom-viewer` server must extract the data from the loom file and convert it to JSON. This is a relatively slow process, and on top of that h5py does not like it when an HDF5 file is opened by multiple processes (even in read-only mode). So to mitigate this issue, whenever JSON data is generated it is also cached as a zipped static file. The next time someone requests that data, the static file is served instead of repeating the whole process. 

The problems start if the data in the loom file is modified (for example, when a column attribute is added to loom file). At this point, existing JSON files that are outdated have to be replaced. It is currently not possible to detect when to do this automatically - it needs to be done manually by whomever is modifying the loom file.

One way around this would be to have a global attribute, or multiple attributes, that are used to keep track of real file changes. Being able to distinguish different kinds of modifications would be nice too. For the `loom-viewer` the following level of precision is enough:

- file metadata, 
- attributes (global, row and column)
- the data matrix as a whole

But perhaps other people have a use for more fine-grained checks (detecting which rows were changed, for example).

I would like to hear the thoughts of others on this, and come up with a shared proposal for how to handle this.

issue_title: Analysis Module: Best Practices Dumpfile
issue_body:
# Detection issue:
Everything commented `// bad` on the [Solidity Recommendations](https://consensys.github.io/smart-contract-best-practices/recommendations/) page.

## Description: 
Provided in link above.

## Link
^

## Implementation details:Initial implementation ideas/instruction
Mythril already catches all of the Solidity problems mentioned in the link above.  I recommend additionally autogenerating a dump file that clumps best practices excerpts with the associated problems.

So it would look like:

`$ myth calls.sol -x`
Which returns this:
```
==== Message call to external contract ====
Type: Informational
Contract: Caller
Function name: thisisfine()
PC address: 691
This contract executes a message call to to another contract. Make sure that the called contract is trusted and does not execute user-supplied code.
--------------------
In file: solidity_examples/calls.sol:16

fixed_address.call()

--------------------
```
and then gives this message:
```
Total Errors: 1
Errors along with associated best practices have been dumped to calls_sol_analysis.html
```
which then would contain everything under the "External Calls" section [here](https://consensys.github.io/smart-contract-best-practices/recommendations/) and group similar errors/mistakes together. 

So if `Total Errors:` returned `16` and three of those were "External Calls", then the `myth -x` output would dump along with the associated best practices excerpt(s) pertaining to those three, and then repeating group/dump, group/dump errors for each error type out of the total of `16`.

To keep the HTML document from getting too cluttered with text, an auto-generated table of contents at the top could link a single heading to each associated group of errors.

issue_title: Using EXCEPTION_HANDLER is not compatible with multiple DRF configurations
issue_body:
To get proper error responses EXCEPTION_HANDLER must be set as mentioned in #188 however EXCEPTION_HANDLER is the only setting that can't be set on a class, thus preventing multi-configuration projects -  I have vanilla-DRF and JSONApi views in the project.

The source of the problem is in https://github.com/tomchristie/django-rest-framework/blob/master/rest_framework/views.py#L413 where exception_handler = self.settings.EXCEPTION_HANDLER

I asked on DRF irc channel about it and it was suggested that the renderer itself could handle that error response formatting. Can't say how that would fit, but it's either that or slight modification to the base view class in DRF so that it's easier to change the exception handler (instead of hacking with deepcopy of self.settings or copy-pasting the whole method).

issue_title: expose constants "pi", "e", in java/C#/python wrappers
issue_body:
Currently there is no good way to to access the `pi` and `e` constants from the managed-language API wrappers.

Speaking only to the java API:

[api_ref.cpp](https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/blob/7861cfcef28de2b2a71166da3be6d1c758ce5a04/src/api/api_rcf.cpp#L79) exposes to java a `long rcfMkPi(long var0)`, with public visibility. There are none of the traditional wrapper functions in `Context` that adapt this method from pointer-values to the traditional `Expr` java object wrappers one would expect. 

This means you either have to deal with pointer values yourself in an entirely un-idiomatic way, or you have to try and invoke the wrappers yourself with reflection. 

Alternatively, one can likely achieve the same ends by only using the SMT-lib (lisp) api. 

---

_to the latter idea_, my failed work-around can be found [here](https://gist.github.com/Groostav/a7aa6e0729734f6f94889c034074700b). Given the time pressures I'm under I will continue to hack my way around with reflection.

issue_title: Optimization: odd behaviour
issue_body:
Not sure if this is my nitpicking again or not.
This is a simple problem, and this source code gives correct results:

    from z3 import *

    # Pablo buys popsicles for his friends. The store sells single popsicles for $1 each, 
    # 3-popsicle boxes for $2, and 5-popsicle boxes for $3. 
    # What is the greatest number of popsicles that Pablo can buy with $8?
    # http://artofproblemsolving.com/wiki/index.php?title=2017_AMC_12A_Problems/Problem_1

    box1pop, box3pop, box5pop = Ints('box1pop box3pop box5pop')
    pop_total = Int('pop_total')
    cost_total = Int('cost_total')

    s=Optimize()

    s.add(pop_total == box1pop*1 + box3pop*3 + box5pop*5)
    s.add(cost_total == box1pop*1 + box3pop*2 + box5pop*3)

    s.add(cost_total==8)

    s.add(box1pop>=0)
    s.add(box3pop>=0)
    s.add(box5pop>=0)

    s.maximize(pop_total)

    print s.check()
    print s.model()

Correct solution:

    sat
    [box3pop = 1,
     box5pop = 2,
     cost_total = 8,
     pop_total = 13,
     box1pop = 0]

However, if to remove all 3 "boxXpop>=0" constraints, z3 (4.6.0) goes crazy:

    sat
    [box3pop = 0,
     box5pop = 0,
     cost_total = 8,
     pop_total = 8,
     box1pop = 8]

So these additional constraints are really necessary?

issue_title: reduce_args tactic produces models with a default value only
issue_body:
The reduce_args tactic's model converter isn't playing very nicely. See the following example:

```Python
from z3 import *
f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())

s = AndThen(Tactic('reduce-args'), Tactic('smt')).solver()
s.add(Or(f(1) == 2, f(2) == 3))
s.check()
print s.model()

s = Tactic('smt').solver()
s.add(Or(f(1) == 2, f(2) == 3))
s.check()
print s.model()
```

Outputs:
```
[f = [else -> If(Var(0) == 2, 0, 2)]]
[f = [1 -> 2, 2 -> 0, else -> 2]]
```

It would be nicer to get the map as we do without using the tactic, since then the API works properly (rather than potentially having to parse the "default" expression).

issue_title: int2bv trivially confused
issue_body:
I'm working on creating a python script symbolic executor and using Z3 as the solver. I've run into a few issues, one of the bigger right now is that int2bv can apparently be trivially confused when the bitvec size is high enough. Here's an example:

```python
import z3

def z3_int_to_bv(x,size=64):
    assert type(x) in [z3.IntNumRef,z3.ArithRef]
    assert x.is_int()
    # Converts Int to BV
    return z3.BitVecRef(z3.Z3_mk_int2bv(x.ctx_ref(),size,x.as_ast()))


a,b = z3.Ints('a b')
c = z3.BitVec('c',64)

s = z3.Solver()

s.add(a == 1)
s.add(b == a)
s.add(c == z3_int_to_bv(b,64) ^ z3.BitVecVal(1,64))
```

For this, the size of 64 basically never returns. It starts getting slow at about a bit length of 16 even though there's only one assignment of this variable previously and it was to a static variable itself. I chose the value of 1 for the sake of demonstration, however it's obviously possible the value would be something else in practice.

How can I get by this problem? For various reasons I really do have to leverage the int2bv function and in some cases it works fine. The case mentioned above does come up a fair amount in practice.

From the [doc](http://lasagne.readthedocs.org/en/latest/modules/init.html#lasagne.init.Glorot), [source](https://github.com/Lasagne/Lasagne/blob/master/lasagne/init.py#L103), it says:

```{python}
For a :class:`DenseLayer <lasagne.layers.DenseLayer>`, if ``gain='relu'``
    and ``initializer=Uniform``, the weights are initialized as
    .. math::
       a &= \\sqrt{\\frac{6}{fan_{in}+fan_{out}}}\\\\
       W &\sim U[-a, a]
```
But from what I read from the souce, the formula is true for a sigmoid activator. In case of Relu, it should be `U[-sqrt(2)a, sqrt(2)a]`, as the gain is sqrt(2).

issue_title: Lasagne/Theano model running much slower than the same model using Caffe
issue_body:
I used to use Caffe for my research. Now I want to use Theano based framework since it is very flexible. I choose to use Lasagne now, it is a wonderful library. But one problem bothers me very long time, the running speed of Lasagne is much slower than Caffe. 

I will show this problem by one of my own project which use AlexNet to do regression. The output layer is a scalar and I use Mean Absolute Error as loss function.
I define the network using Lasagne which is similar to the one in Lasagne’s model zoo (https://github.com/Lasagne/Recipes/blob/master/modelzoo/caffe_reference.py).

I use the same learning rate, weight decay, momentum as the Caffe model.

I define my ```iterate_minibatches``` function to read a batch of images and labels from a text file (one line of this text file contains the path of image and label) which is similar to the function at https://github.com/Lasagne/Recipes/blob/master/papers/deep_residual_learning/Deep_Residual_Learning_CIFAR-10.py#L157

Note that, for Caffe I also define a python data layer (without preloading) which is nearly the same as ```iterate_minibatches```, so I think the data loading and preprocessing time is the same for Caffe and Lasagne code. 

But the training speed is very different, 20 iterations take 12 seconds for Caffe, while it takes 46 seconds for Lasagne.  

The code of my Lasagne model definition and tranining procedure at here  https://gist.github.com/kli-nlpr/41ec0eb567b09f29299958e9d3455eef

The training log of Lasagne model at here https://gist.github.com/kli-nlpr/14d94398d9157fafb0ba5ec778c7d536

The training log of Caffe model at here https://gist.github.com/kli-nlpr/3d57c9797538ed15e3647327f644a043

The profile of Lasagne code at here https://gist.github.com/kli-nlpr/4fd1d6cf2a90ff584fe4177c43e2991a

I really appreciate your help and suggestions, Thanks.

By default, the Jupyter installed from Anaconda works just fine. A little research (sparked by trying to get the nteract app working) shows that there are *no* `kernel.json` files anywhere on the system; thus no joy for the nteract app.

A simple (but completely unknown to me) `ipython kernel install` from the command prompt sufficed to get things working (this generates a `kernel.json` and drops it into `C:\ProgramData\jupyter\kernels\python3`).

issue_title: URL property return None
issue_body:
Hi guys,

I installed your app yesterday and since that I am still trying to get it work. I have a big trouble with getting correct URL for all my pages. Page.url return None instead of correct URL string. I found that the problem is in cache mechanism.

These steps folow my problem:
1) start webserver
2) http://localhost:7000/cs/
3) all links to subpages are OK
4) switching language to "en" ... http://localhost:7000/en/
5) all links to subpages are broken = http://localhost:7000/en/None
6) since that "cs" is OK, "en" is not OK till the cache is rebuild

```   @property
    def url(self):
        root_paths = Site.get_site_root_paths()
        for (id, root_path, root_url) in root_paths:
            if self.url_path.startswith(root_path):
                return ('' if len(root_paths) == 1 else root_url) + reverse('wagtail_serve', args=(self.url_path[len(root_path):],))
```

I found where is the problem. In the Page.url ...  Site.get_site_root_paths() always return the root_path of the first initialized language (f.e. "cs"). That is because get_site_root_paths is using cache here:
```    @staticmethod
    def get_site_root_paths():
        result = cache.get('wagtail_site_root_paths')
        return result
```
I know couple workarounds howto figure it out. But I don't think that is is OK.

issue_title: Not working when defining a custom edit_handler
issue_body:
wagtail-modeltranslation 0.5.0a1

When you define a custom edit_handler ([look here](http://docs.wagtail.io/en/v1.6.2/advanced_topics/customisation/page_editing_interface.html?highlight=edit_handlers#customising-the-tabbed-interface)) in a model with any translatable field, things go wrong: you don't see any translatable field in the editor window, like if the model wasn't a translatable one.

The next sample works correctly only if the edit_handler definition is kept commented:

```
class TestPage(TranslationMixin, Page):
    body = StreamField(FullStreamBlock())

    content_panels = Page.content_panels + [
        FieldPanel('title', classname="full title"),
        StreamFieldPanel('body'),
    ]
    
    #It doesn't work if the next is uncommented!!!

#         ObjectList(Page.promote_panels, heading='Promote'),
#         ObjectList(Page.settings_panels, heading='Settings', classname="settings"),
#     ])
    
    search_fields = Page.search_fields + (
        index.SearchField('body'),
    )
```

translation.py

```
from wagtail_modeltranslation.translation import TranslationOptions
from wagtail_modeltranslation.decorators import register

from .models import TestPage

@register(TestPage)
class TestPageTR(TranslationOptions):
    fields = (
        'body',
    )
```

issue_title: Question: Caller-Simprocedure
issue_body:
Hello, not a bug this time around - just a short question.

I want to skip indirect-calls in ARM32, e.g. `BLX R3` with R3 being an address only known at runtime. 

A working solution is 

```python
project.hook(addr=0x400e5b, hook=blx, length=2)

def indirect_blx_hook(state):
    if state.addr % 2 == 0:
        state.regs.r14 = state.regs.pc + 4 # set LR
    else:
        state.regs.r14 = state.regs.pc + 2 # set LR
```

This method requires an additional length parameter in `hook` which, ofc has to be `thumb/arm` sensitive.

My question is, if it is possible to address this more generically with the Stubs proecudure 'Caller' which gets the return unconstrained hook as target. Or any other more appropriate solution. Couldn't get it to work

Greetings,
Andy

UnicodeDecodeError: 'utf-32-be' codec can't decode bytes in position 0-3: code point not in range(0x110000)
```

#### Problem description

Digging through the source code, the problem seems to be with the use of `np.str` https://github.com/pytroll/satpy/blob/99c2f7269a40cb17ea661cb25db419e48b57d105/satpy/readers/native_msg_hdr.py#L170.

`np.str` seems to make data be read in `utf-32`:

```python
data = b'some data'
dt = np.dtype((np.str, 1))
print(np_str_dt.itemsize)
# 4
np.frombuffer(data, dtype=dt, count=1)
# raises same error: 
# UnicodeDecodeError: 'utf-32-le' codec can't decode bytes in position 0-3: code point not in range(0x110000)
```
replacing with `'|S{num_bytes}'` and things work as expected:
```python
data = b'some data'
dt = np.dtype('|S1')
dt.itemsize
# 1
np.frombuffer(data, dtype=dt, count=1)
# array([b's'], dtype='|S1')
```

#### Versions of Python, package at hand and relevant dependencies
Python 3.6, satpy 0.8.1

issue_title: test api 不正确
issue_body:
`
def test_get_order():
    from rqalpha.api import order_shares, get_order
    def init(context):
        context.s1 = '000001.XSHE'
        context.amount = 100

    def handle_bar(context, bar_dict):
        order_id = order_shares(context.s1, context.amount, style=LimitOrder(9.5))
        order = get_order(order_id)
        assert order.order_book_id == context.s1
        assert order.quantity == context.amount
        assert order.unfilled_quantity + order.filled_quantity == order.quantity
test_get_order_code_new = "".join(inspect.getsourcelines(test_get_order)[0])
`
最后得到的 test_get_order_code_new 仅定义了 test_get_order 函数(init 及 handle_bar 并没有定义)，这个代码传到 rqalpha 中跑完全是空转，没有达到测试目的。

issue_title: Support for Python 3.6
issue_body:
### Version of the SDK being used
```bash
$ pip freeze | grep face
facebook-sdk==2.0.0
```

### Expected Behavior
```python
>>> import facebook
>>> # the next line should not raise an error
>>> facebook.GraphAPI
>>> # yay!
```

### Actual behavior
```python
>>> import facebook
>>> # the next line should not raise an error
>>> facebook.GraphAPI
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
AttributeError: module 'facebook' has no attribute 'GraphAPI'
```

### Steps to Reproduce
This *only* happens in Python 3.6. Watch this:

```bash
$ workon my-35-virtualenv
(my-35-virtualenv) $ python --version
3.5.2
(my-35-virtualenv) $ python
>>> import facebook
>>> facebook.GraphAPI
>>> # yay!
>>> exit(0)
(my-35-virtualenv) $ workon my-36-virtualenv
(my-36-virtualenv) $ python --version
3.6.1
(my-36-virtualenv) $ python
>>> import facebook
>>> facebook.GraphAPI
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
AttributeError: module 'facebook' has no attribute 'GraphAPI'
```

This leads me to believe that python 3.6 changed how imports work. I'll do some research on 3.6 imports, but I wanted this issue to be known. From what I saw there aren't any issues here nor on the facebook developer forum that address this.

issue_title: What are the differences between the two r200 Models
issue_body:
Can anyone tell me the difference between the two r200 developer kits

- Model # :946432 - $99 USD
- Model # :VF0830 - Price: $169.00  

My use case is to use multiple cameras for object scanning in relatively close range 1-2 meters. Assuming both will work with librealsense, what differentiates these 2 models?

Thanks

issue_body:

issue_title: Fix the json generation of SomethingLike
issue_body:
Hello, 

There is a small issue when we try to generate those kind of interactions:
```
SomethingLike({
   'username': Term('.+', 'admin'),
   'email': ''
})
```

The problem is in file `pact/matchers.py` on line 120:
```
return {
  'json_class': 'Pact::SomethingLike',
  'contents': self.matcher}
```
https://github.com/pact-foundation/pact-python/blob/master/pact/matchers.py#L120

It should be 
```
return {
  'json_class': 'Pact::SomethingLike',
  'contents': from_term(self.matcher)}
```
This fix should manage when `self.matcher` is a python dictionary.

issue_title: Can't use a Union type in a connection
issue_body:
If I have something like this:

```python
class SomeTypeOne(ObjectType):
    pass

class SomeTypeTwo(ObjectType):
    pass

class MyUnion(Union):
    class Meta:
        types = (SomeTypeOne, SomeTypeTwo)

class Query(AbstractType):
    things = relay.ConnectionField(MyUnion)
```

it fails with `AssertionError: Only ObjectTypes can have a Node interface.`  If the last line is changed to:

```python
things = graphene.Field(MyUnion)
```

then it works fine.

Is there another way to do this, or is it just not supported yet?

issue_title: Explicit registration of object types
issue_body:
I'm not sure if the answer to this is "I'm doing it wrong" but here we go.

## Problem

So one thing I want to do is to be able to get information about the current user from the API. I have an `AnonNode` and a `UserNode` which both implement `UserInterface`. I think add a field `current_user = graphene.Field(UserInterface)` with an appropriate resolver. `UserNode` is also explicitly registered in other places, but `AnonNode` is not, I'm not using it for anything other that this specific field. If I query the field as an anonymous user, I get the following error:

```
Runtime Object type "AnonNode" is not a possible type for "UserInterface".
```

If I explicitly register the `AnonNode` by adding a meaningless field anywhere in my schema which uses that type directly, everything works.

I was hoping to be able to use `schema.register(AnonNode)`, but this seems to only update `graphene.Schema` and not the underlying `graphql.Schema`. The new type becomes present in `my_schema.type_map` but not `my_schema.schema.type_map`. The latter appears to be largely immutable.

## Possible solutions
- Deal with having to have every type associated directly with a field, but document that in the interfaces docs
- Update `schema.register` to allow explicit registration of arbitrary object types.
- Add some other API for explicit registration.
- Update `graphene.Field` to give it an extra `implementations` arg or similar when registering an `Interface` (this could  - anything matching that interface should be valid).

issue_title: Limit word length - Skipgram
issue_body:
I am using FastText to compute skipgrams on a corpus containing a long sequence of characters with no spaces. After an hour or so, FastText produces a model containing vectors (of length 100) corresponding to "words" of length 50 characters from the corpus.

I tried setting -minn and -maxn parameters, but that does not help (I kind of knew it won't, but tried anyway), and -wordNgrams parameter only applies if there are spaces, I guess (?!). This one is just a long stream of characters representing state, without spaces.

The documentation doesn't seem to have any information on this (or perhaps I'm missing something?). I was wondering if perhaps someone has been through this or something similar, where you had to limit the size of the words corresponding to an output vector?

issue_title: python2 broken on reading existing config
issue_body:
When using python2.7 for 0.0.19, I get:

```
me@tak.local ~ $ aws-google-auth -a --resolve-aliases 
Failed to import U2F libraries, U2F login unavailable. Other methods can still continue.
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/bin/aws-google-auth", line 11, in <module>
    load_entry_point('aws-google-auth==0.0.19', 'console_scripts', 'aws-google-auth')()
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/aws_google_auth/__init__.py", line 54, in main
    cli(sys.argv[1:])
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/aws_google_auth/__init__.py", line 79, in cli
    config.read(config.profile)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/aws_google_auth/configuration.py", line 182, in read
    read_ask_role = config_parser[profile_string].getboolean('google_config.ask_role', None)
AttributeError: RawConfigParser instance has no attribute '__getitem__'
```

because the python3 syntax for `RawConfigParser` to get a whole section via `config_parser['section name']` is not available in the native python2.7 `configparser`

A version-independent syntax would be `config_parser.getboolean(profile_string, item)` or `config_parser.getint(profile_string, item)`

issue_title: Missing Option to Prevent Processing of CLI Args
issue_body:
Originally reported by: **Tyler Abair (Bitbucket: [tabair](http://bitbucket.org/tabair), GitHub: [tabair](http://github.com/tabair))**

---

Cmd2 has a cool feature where it will process all CLI args passed to a script as commands, but there is no option to turn this feature off.

A quick work-around that I'm using at the moment is to save sys.argv to a tmp variable, and then set sys.argv = [tmp[0]] just before calling Cmd.cmdloop() and restore it after, but it'd be much nicer if I could just set an instance variable on my Cmd object to turn this feature off instead.

---
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/catherinedevlin/cmd2/issue/14

issue_title: How do we expect people to use hooks?
issue_body:
I think pre, post, and renew hooks in Certbot are quite complicated and hard to use. There's a number of rules for when each hooks is run, what options overwrite previously defined hooks, and options for users where to define their hooks:

* renewal configuration files
* global configuration file
* command line
* `*-hook.d` directories in the Ubuntu PPA

We should try to come up with a clear picture for what we expect users to do here and guide them through the process. I think we should also try to simplify things where possible without breaking existing behavior.

issue_title: --force-renew vs --force-renewal
issue_body:
hello i would like to make a small bug report regarding the manual.

inside the text of the manual there is a passage:

The --force-renew flag may be helpful for automating renewal; it causes the expiration time of the certificate(s) to be ignored when considering renewal, and attempts to renew each and every installed certificate regardless of its age. (This form is not appropriate to run daily because each certificate will be renewed every day, which will quickly run into the certificate authority rate limit.)

if continued to scroll down however, one can see that there is no "--force-renew" but only a "--force-renewal" parameter.

  --force-renewal, --renew-by-default
                        If a certificate already exists for the requested
                        domains, renew it now, regardless of whether it is
                        near expiry. (Often --keep-until-expiring is more
                        appropriate). Also implies --expand. (default: False)

it confused at least me so much that i now use --renew-by-default instead of the other one(s). maybe someone wanna fix that. thank you for your attention.

issue_title: HelpfulArgumentParser should know about flags that are relevant to several topics
issue_body: We currently have a bunch of hairy special cases where flags are documented in different places depending on which help topics are being displayed.  These are bad to maintain, so we should either have HelpfulArgumentParser know about and organise them automatically (alternatively, we chould completely rearchitect cli.py to give good concise topic-based help in some better way, such as writing our own argument parser, forking [config]argparse in the tree, or something else..).

issue_title: Certbot Automatically Create pem File that Contains Both privkey.pem and fullchain.pem in One
issue_body:
## Certificate with Both privkey.pem and fullchain.pem

Would it be possible for certbot to automatically create a pem certificate file that contains both the privkey and fullchain in one pem file when creating a certificate for the first time or when a certificate is renewed?

Programs like courier, lighttpd, and vsftpd use certificates in this format.

Usually, I have to run a command to manually combine both certificates like this:

`cat /etc/letsencrypt/live/test.com/privkey.pem /etc/letsencrypt/live/test.com/fullchain.pem > /etc/letsencrypt/live/test.com/courier.pem && /usr/sbin/service courier-pop-ssl restart && /usr/sbin/service courier-imap-ssl restart`

By default, certbot creates cert.pem, chain.pem, fullchain.pem, and privkey.pem files for each domain. Perhaps another one called mixed.pem could be created (and updated when renewed) by default? This would be ideal because then I don't have to manually combine them.

https://community.letsencrypt.org/t/certbot-pem-automatic-creation-create-pem-file-that-contains-both-privkey-pem-and-fullchain-pem-in-one/60881

#### My operating system is Ubuntu 16.04 x64

#### I installed Certbot with certbot-auto
